---

#### 5. Red-Flag Severity Verdict

**Material concerns.**

There are no Critical flags — no going-concern, fraud, auditor-resignation, or covenant-breach trigger, and the FY25 audit opinion is unmodified — so the earnings setup is not invalidated outright. But ten High-severity flags cluster on one fault line: the earnings setup is genuine and improving at EBITDA, yet weak, distorted, and decelerating below EBITDA, and the market narrative (rising target prices) is running ahead of a bottom line the Street is actively cutting (FY27 EPS −19% in six months). The single most dangerous red flag is the EBITDA-up / PAT-down divergence (reported PAT ₹482M → ₹444M → ₹138M while EBITDA rose 20%): it means "earnings accelerating" is true only at the operating line and false at the bottom line, so any synthesis verdict must specify the metric or it will mislead. What would resolve it: the FY26 audited statutory statements plus an FQ1 2027 print (release 30 Jul 2026) showing revenue re-accelerating toward the ~15% guide AND D&A/finance cost/tax landing where the cut estimates now sit — i.e., a clean, exceptional-free PAT that converts the EBITDA gain into bottom-line earnings.

---

#### 6. What The Synthesis Agent Should Know

- 47 red flags triggered (0 Critical, 10 High, 28 Medium, 9 Low) plus 2 Unclear; no Critical means no automatic verdict-lock, but the High cluster supports a "Material concerns" overlay.
- Single most dangerous flag: **EBITDA-up / PAT-down divergence** — reported PAT fell ₹482M → ₹444M → ₹138M (FY24→FY26) while EBITDA rose +20% in FY26 `[01 output, §1, §6]`; reported FY26 EPS ₹1.0 is not a usable run-rate (adjusted PAT ₹557M is +305% of reported) `[06 output, §7]`.
- The earnings verdict should NOT be "Earnings accelerating" unqualified. The evidence supports **"Mixed earnings setup"** or, at most, "accelerating at EBITDA / decelerating at PAT" — the EBITDA line accelerates, but revenue decelerates intra-year (Q1 +16.6% → Q4 +11.5%), the bottom line is down and distorted, and EPS revisions are net-negative.
- Score caps the synthesis should apply (from MODULE_RULES): (1) **Earnings-volatility confidence must be Low** — `07_earnings-sensitivity` is missing and all sensitivities are inferred; the inverted earnings-volatility score cannot be presented as high-confidence. (2) Beat/miss setup confidence is degraded — `05_beat-miss-setup` is missing; treat category 2.6 as reconstructed, not authoritative. (3) Consensus setup is NOT capped (full CIQ consensus/revisions/surprise present), but should reflect the net-negative revision breadth.
- Contradictions to reconcile: "01 Stable" vs "02 decelerating" revenue (adopt decelerating intra-year); "TP raised vs EPS cut" (a narrative flag, not a resolved bull signal); deck ROCE 14% vs statutory ~4.6% (use statutory); capital-allocation severity 35 vs red-flags-sweep 55 on the ₹3.3bn minority puts (use 55, conservative). The ₹3.3bn bank-dependent minority put obligation `[BM 12, §3]` is the cross-module item the earnings module is silent on but that competes with FY27 expansion capex for the same constrained cash — relevant to whether the growth thesis is self-fundable.
- Missing data that prevented a full scan: `05_beat-miss-setup`, `07_earnings-sensitivity`, FY26 audited statutory statements, quarterly consensus surprise series, per-modality/payor gross margins, and the post-KKR-changeover Reg-31 shareholding/promoter-pledge line.
- The setup is **dirtier than the EBITDA-led upstream framing suggests, but not fraudulent**: the distortions (PAT exceptionals, adjusted-vs-reported gap, deck-tier FY26, edit-log control gap, mislabeled labour-code "one-off") are transparent and disclosed — the issue is fragility and narrative overreach, not concealment. Reported earnings are fragile, not inflated.

---

#### 7. Pre-Mortem — If The Earnings Setup Fails

If the earnings setup proves wrong, the most likely reason we missed it is that we let the EBITDA story stand in for the earnings story — the +20% EBITDA growth and +88 bps margin gain are real and cash-backed, but they sit on top of a bottom line that is structurally thin (FY26 PBT pre-exceptional was a fraction of revenue), exceptional-distorted (reported PAT ₹138M vs adjusted ₹557M), and exposed to forces below EBITDA — D&A from the capex build-out, finance cost, a noisy tax line, a recurring labour-code charge dressed as a one-off, and the bank-funding-dependent minority puts — exactly where the Street has missed EPS in three of the last four years and is cutting FY27 estimates now (flags #1, #5, #6, #27). The trap is that a reader anchors on the accelerating EBITDA and the rising sell-side target prices, models the bottom line off them, and is then surprised when FY27 PAT once again lands below consensus because the operating gain never made it through depreciation, interest, tax and exceptionals to EPS — the precise failure mode the four-year surprise record already telegraphs.

---

# PART IV — Balance-Sheet-Survival · FULL MODULE (synthesis + 7 specialist reports)

*Folder: `analyses/HCG_2026-06-01/balance-sheet-survival/`. The module synthesis appears first (the module's own adjudicated verdict), followed by each specialist report in pipeline order, reproduced in full.*

## BSS-99 · Module Synthesis — Balance-Sheet-Survival Module — HCG (Synthesis)

### Balance-Sheet-Survival Module — HCG (Synthesis)

#### Abstract

HealthCare Global (HCG) carries moderate leverage that fell sharply in FY26 — net debt 2.57x EBITDA including capitalised leases (0.80x ex-lease), down from 4.13x, but the deleveraging was funded by a ₹4,250 Mn rights issue, not free cash flow. There is no bullet maturity: the debt is a flat ~₹1.0–1.3 Bn/yr secured term-loan ladder plus a long, self-amortising lease tail, and the dated term maturity inside 12 months (~₹1.3 Bn) is covered ~4x by ₹5,360.5 Mn of cash. Runway is ~15 months on maintenance capex; no lender covenant is disclosed, so headroom is Not assessable. Liquidity survives a 60% EBITDA fall if the on-demand overdraft rolls; an assumed coverage covenant would break first, near −24%. Verdict: Adequate.

#### 1. Solvency Verdict

- **Verdict:** **Adequate**
- **Net leverage (net debt / EBITDA):** **2.57x incl. leases** (cash only), **0.80x ex-lease** (cash only), on FY26 Reported EBITDA ₹4,657.9 Mn; company-headline ~0.73x (ex-lease, margin-money-netted) and 1.43x (Vizag-adjusted). Gross debt incl. leases / EBITDA = **3.73x** [01 §5, §7].
- **Liquidity runway:** **~15 months** on a maintenance-capex basis (cash ₹5,360.5 Mn vs net burn ₹4,250.9 Mn/yr); ~31 months on cash and FCF-covered once the ~₹2.8 Bn on-demand overdraft is treated as rolled; ~10.8 months as the conservative outer bound (full overdraft repaid + all growth capex spent) [03 §3].
- **Maturity wall (% within 24 months):** **46.7%** of borrowings (FY25 undiscounted); within 12 months **34.2%** ex-lease — but **~57% of that is the rollable on-demand overdraft**, so the dated term maturity inside 12 months is **~14.6% (~₹1.3 Bn)**; 21.7% within 12 months including leases [02 §1–2].
- **Tightest covenant + headroom:** **Not assessable** — no lender financial maintenance covenant is disclosed in the pool. The only quantified anchor is management's self-imposed 2.5x ex-lease net-leverage ceiling, against which leverage sits ~68% below [04 §2–3].
- **Stress break point (EBITDA decline that breaks it):** **~−24%** — an *assumed* 2.0x lease-inclusive interest-coverage covenant would breach first (shallower than a normal recession), but it is a labeled assumption, not a disclosed covenant. Liquidity barely breaks on earnings alone (~−85% with the overdraft rolled); the next real cliff is the ~₹2.8 Bn on-demand overdraft being *called* (a counterparty action), which opens a ~₹0.6–1.3 Bn 12-month gap in a −40% to −60% downside [06 §3–4].
- Solvency strength /100: **62** (capped at 70 — no clean maturity schedule)
- Liquidity runway /100: **58** (capped at 60 — no undrawn-facility / revolver-availability disclosure)
- Refinancing risk /100 *(higher = WORSE — inverted)*: **40** (confidence Low — no year-by-year schedule at the FY26 date)
- Covenant headroom /100: **Not assessable** (no covenant disclosed)
- Downside resilience /100: **60**
- Data quality /100: **52** (from 00 — Partial)
- Overall usefulness /100: **72** (capped at 75 — no covenant disclosure)
- Biggest solvency risk (one line): **The combination of an undisclosed lender coverage covenant that could trip near a −24% EBITDA fall and an undisclosed-availability ~₹2.8 Bn on-demand overdraft that, if pulled, opens a near-term cash gap — both sit outside what the EBITDA haircut alone controls.**

#### 2. Specialist Roll-Up

| Specialist | Verdict Line | Biggest Finding |
|---|---|---|
| solvency-data-triage | Sufficiency: **Partial** — balance sheet, debt note, cash flows present; covenants, clean maturity table, facility availability, ratings absent | No covenant thresholds, no year-by-year maturity table, no undrawn-facility availability → three caps bind (solvency max 70, covenant Not assessable, liquidity max 60). (Note: stated "no rating in pool" — corrected in §3.) |
| capital-structure-and-leverage | Net debt **2.57x incl. leases / 0.80x ex-lease**; leverage rose to 4.13x in FY25 then fell, **equity-funded not cash-funded** | Management leads with ex-lease ~0.73–1.4x; the like-for-like lease-inclusive figure is 2.57x. ~96% floating, largely unhedged, ~8% cost. ICRA A+ (Stable)/A1+ rating found in the AR. |
| maturity-wall-and-refinancing | **"Self-funded / low refi risk"** for dated term maturities; confidence capped Low | No bullet — flat ~₹1.0–1.3 Bn/yr term ladder + long lease tail; refi cost steps *down* ~50 bps (repo at 5.25%); residual risks are the unhedged floating book and the on-demand overdraft roll. |
| liquidity-runway | **~15 months** maintenance-capex runway; ~31 months / FCF-covered overdraft-rolled; **liquidity = cash only (understated)** | Single biggest liquidity risk is the undisclosed committed-facility availability — the ~₹2.8 Bn on-demand overdraft could be pulled exactly when needed. ~70% of runway is in-hand cash. |
| coverage-and-covenants | EBITDA/interest **2.64x** (lease-incl) / ~4.8x cash-interest; **covenant headroom Not assessable** | No lender covenant disclosed; an *assumed* 2.0x coverage covenant would trip at ~−24% EBITDA. (EBITDA−capex)/interest at break-even 1.00x driven by heavy growth capex; cash conversion eroding (CFO/EBITDA 74.5%). |
| off-balance-sheet-and-contingencies | Largest items are **Vizag put/forward (~₹2.0 Bn recognised, ~₹3.3 Bn gross)** + **₹517.6 Mn EPCG**; max exposure ~50.5% of equity | ₹1,543 Mn Vizag tranche already crystallised (paid Apr-2026); two parent obligations (BG/CC guarantee, letter of support to 13 subs) disclosed but unquantified → solvency strength cap 75. |
| downside-stress-test | **Liquidity survives a 30–60% EBITDA fall** if the overdraft rolls; **assumed coverage covenant breaks first at ~−24%** | Liquidity does not break on earnings alone (~−85% rolled); the real cliffs are (1) the overdraft being called and (2) an undisclosed coverage covenant near −24%; a −30/−40% recession needs no equity/asset sale to survive on liquidity. |

#### 3. Reconciliation

**Disagreement 1 — Credit rating (material).** The triage (`00`) lists credit ratings as **absent** ("No rating-agency report or rating reference in pool"). Agents `01`, `02`, and `04` independently cite **ICRA A+ (Stable) long-term / A1+ short-term**, sourced to the FY24-25 Annual Report, Corporate Governance Report, "O. Credit Ratings", p.~155, and MD&A p.174, with no revision during FY25. **Reconciled view:** a rating *reference* IS in the pool (the AR discloses the ICRA ratings); what is absent is a full rating-agency *report*. The investment-grade-equivalent domestic rating is accepted as the more-evidenced finding (three agents, specific page citations). It supports domestic refinancing access but does not lift the partial-data caps, because the triage's substantive point stands — no rating-agency report means rating *detail*, triggers, and outlook drivers cannot be examined. The "no credit ratings" cap (do not infer a rating) is therefore moot: a real, cited rating exists; it is noted, not inferred.

**Disagreement 2 — Headline leverage basis (presentation, not a number conflict).** Management and the decks lead with ex-lease, margin-money-netted leverage of ~0.73–1.43x; `01` computes 2.57x on a like-for-like lease-inclusive basis. These are not contradictory — they are different bases. Per the module's conservative default, the **lease-inclusive 2.57x is the anchor** used throughout this synthesis and by the stress agent; the ex-lease figure is shown alongside, not buried.

**Disagreement 3 — 12-month maturity figure (resolved by labeling).** `03` (which ran in parallel before `02` landed) used the full ₹3,112.2 Mn current-borrowings line as the conservative 12-month figure; `02` clarified that ~57% (~₹1.78 Bn) is the rollable on-demand overdraft, leaving ~₹1.3 Bn of genuinely dated term maturity. Both readings are carried explicitly: the ~₹1.3 Bn dated figure for the refinancing read, the full ₹3.1 Bn as the conservative liquidity-uses bound. No conflict — the conservative reading is the outer bound, not the central case.

No other material disagreements between specialists.

#### 3A. Fragility Map (what breaks first)

| Fragility Driver | Indicator | Current Status | Why It Matters |
|---|---|---|---|
| Maturity concentration | % due within 24m | **46.7%** of borrowings (FY25 undisc.); but <1yr bucket ~70% rollable overdraft, no single-year bullet spike; dated term ladder ~₹1.0–1.3 Bn/yr | Low wall risk — the concentration is a self-amortising lease tail and a rolled working-capital line, not a refinanceable bullet [02 §2] |
| Availability liquidity | usable liquidity vs uses | Cash ₹5,360.5 Mn (revolver/cash-credit **availability undisclosed → excluded**) vs hard 12m uses ~₹4.3 Bn | The revolver/cash-credit backstop is unknown; headline liquidity is cash-only and **understates** true liquidity, but the on-demand overdraft inside the debt could be pulled [03 §1, §5] |
| Covenant illusion risk | covenant EBITDA vs reported | **No lender covenant disclosed**; company's own metrics use Reported/Adjusted EBITDA (gap +1.15%), so addback illusion in *visible* metrics is low | Headroom to any real bilateral-loan covenant is unknown; the survival floor depends on a covenant the pool cannot show [04 §2, "Covenant EBITDA"] |
| Floating-rate sensitivity | floating % net of hedges | **~96% floating, largely unhedged**; +200 bps ≈ −₹175 Mn pre-tax (~3.8% of EBITDA) | Near-term repricing is *downward* (repo 5.25% vs ~6.5% at origination); a rate reversal, not refi cost, is the live risk [02 §3] |
| Structural subordination | HoldCo debt vs upstreaming | Debt is **OpCo-level and secured** with parent guarantees; no HoldCo bond stack; negative consolidated retained earnings preclude dividends | Structural subordination low-to-moderate; the upstreaming cap is **not** fully triggered (debt maps to operating entities) [01 §6A] |
| Contingent accelerants | CoC puts / cross-default | **Not disclosed in the data pool** (KKR took sole control 30 May 2025 via open offer; no debt-side CoC put, cross-default, or rating step disclosed) | A change-of-control trigger, if it existed, could accelerate debt; its absence from disclosure is itself a data gap [04 §2; 05] |

#### 4. Score Cap Application

| Cap Trigger | Applied? (Y/N) | Affected Score | Final Cap |
|---|---|---|---|
| No debt maturity schedule | **Y** (only current/non-current split + qualitative tenor; year-by-year table is FY25, one year stale) | Solvency strength; Refinancing-risk confidence | Solvency strength **max 70** (applied → 62); refi-risk confidence **Low** |
| No covenant disclosure | **Y** (no lender financial covenant in pool) | Covenant headroom; Overall usefulness | Covenant headroom **= Not assessable**; usefulness **max 75** (applied → 72) |
| No cash flow statement | **N** (FY24-25 AR + CapIQ cash flows present) | Liquidity runway | — (not triggered) |
| Only annual data (no interim) | **N** (FY26 audited results deck/transcript provide latest interim-equivalent) | Solvency strength | — (not triggered) |
| No EBITDA base (stress not run) | **N** (FY26 + multi-year EBITDA present; stress ran fully) | Downside resilience | — (not triggered); downside resilience **assessable** |
| Off-balance-sheet exposures undisclosed for a known-levered/acquisitive name | **Y** (BG/CC guarantee + letter of support to 13 subs disclosed but unquantified; FY26 contingencies note absent) | Solvency strength | **max 75** (less restrictive than the maturity-schedule cap) |
| Revolver exists but availability unknown (borrowing base) | **Y** (cash-credit/overdraft availability undisclosed) | Liquidity runway | **max 60** (applied → 58) |
| Covenant headroom relies on assumed covenant-EBITDA addbacks | **N** (no covenant computed at all — Not assessable supersedes) | Covenant headroom | superseded by "Not assessable" |
| HoldCo has material debt but upstreaming constraints unclear | **N** (debt is OpCo-level, secured, with parent guarantees; maps cleanly) | Solvency strength | — (not triggered per 01 §6A) |

Most restrictive on Solvency strength: the **no-maturity-schedule cap (max 70)** binds (more restrictive than the off-balance-sheet max 75). Final solvency strength = 62.

#### 5. Survival Summary

HCG is moderately levered and the trend is improving in headline terms but for a one-time reason: net debt including leases fell from 4.13x to 2.57x EBITDA in FY26, but the fall was driven by a ₹4,250 Mn rights issue and some term-loan repayment, not free cash flow (FY26 FCF was only ~₹586 Mn against ₹2,885 Mn of capex) — it is balance-sheet repair, not organic deleveraging. The near-term maturity wall is **self-funded**: there is no bullet, the dated term maturity inside 12 months is ~₹1.3 Bn against ₹5,360.5 Mn of cash (~4x cover), refinancing cost actually steps *down* at the current 5.25% repo, and the ICRA A+/A1+ rating supports domestic access — the only refinancing-dependent item is the roll of the ~₹2.8 Bn on-demand overdraft, whose committed availability is undisclosed. The liquidity runway is ~15 months on maintenance capex and effectively open-ended (FCF-covered, ~31 months on cash) if that overdraft rolls; the tightest covenant cannot be measured because none is disclosed, so the assessment leans on management's self-imposed 2.5x ex-lease ceiling (~68% headroom) and on labeled-assumption market covenants. On the stress test, the structure first fails — *if* a typical bilateral 2.0x lease-inclusive coverage covenant exists — at only ~−24% EBITDA, shallower than a normal recession, requiring a waiver but no equity or asset sale to survive; on liquidity alone, a normal −30%/−40% recession is comfortably survivable (a ₹1.5–1.9 Bn 12-month surplus) and even a −60% year leaves ~14 months of cash, provided the secured overdraft is not called at the same time.

#### 6. What Would Change The Solvency Verdict?

| Current Verdict | What Would Strengthen It | What Would Weaken It | Data Needed |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Adequate** | A clean year-by-year maturity schedule confirming no bullet (would lift the solvency cap from 70); disclosure of committed undrawn cash-credit availability (would lift liquidity from cash-only and the 60 cap); disclosed lender covenants with wide headroom (turns "Not assessable" into measured headroom); a fresh ICRA rating affirmation/upgrade; organic (cash-funded) deleveraging continuing in FY27 | A disclosed lender coverage covenant near the assumed 2.0x level (would confirm a real break at ~−24%, shallower than a recession → toward Stretched); the on-demand overdraft being pulled or cut; a sustained EBITDA fall >24–30% (DPCO drug-price control + government-scheme payer-mix shock); cash conversion continuing to erode below 74.5%; a large EPCG (₹517.6 Mn) or unquantified guarantee crystallising; further debt-funded M&A re-levering toward the 2.5x ceiling | (1) Lender credit agreement / covenant schedule; (2) committed-facility availability & borrowing base; (3) year-by-year maturity table at the FY26 date; (4) full ICRA rating report with triggers/outlook drivers; (5) FY26 statutory contingencies note |

#### 6A. Survival Playbook (non-speculative levers)

Levers supported by evidence:
- **Equity issuance — demonstrated.** A ₹4,250 Mn rights issue was completed in Q4 FY26 (oversubscribed 1.3x); KKR holds sole control (since 30 May 2025) and runs an internal 2.5x ex-lease leverage ceiling, so a controlling sponsor with a deleveraging discipline is in place [01 §6; 06 §4].
- **Asset-sale program — signed.** The Milann fertility-clinic divestment is signed: EV ₹632 Mn, ~₹282 Mn upfront (75% of ₹376 Mn equity consideration), close expected Q1 FY27, balance ₹94 Mn over 18 months. **Related-party flag:** the buyer (Inviga Healthcare Fund I) is controlled by promoter/Non-Exec Chairman Dr. B.S. Ajaikumar [02 §4].
- **Capex flexibility — large and demonstrated.** ~₹1,696 Mn/yr of the ₹2,885 Mn FY26 capex is discretionary growth capex that can be paused; maintenance capex is only ₹1,189 Mn. Pausing growth capex lifts the runway from ~10.8 to ~15+ months and is the single largest internal lever [03 §2–3; 06 §4].
- **Dividend/buyback suspension — not applicable as a lever.** No dividend has ever been paid (negative consolidated retained earnings of ₹(4,092) Mn legally preclude it), so there is no distribution to cut — but equally no distribution leakage to manage [01 §6A; 03 §2].
- **Covenant-amendment likelihood — not evidenced.** No lender covenant is disclosed, so prior waiver/amendment history cannot be assessed; this lever is unproven from the pool [04 §2].

#### 7. Note To The Final Synthesizer

- **Leverage level and direction:** moderate and falling in headline terms — net debt 2.57x EBITDA incl. leases / 0.80x ex-lease (cash), down from 4.13x in FY25; gross incl. leases 3.73x. The fall was **equity-funded (₹4,250 Mn rights issue), not cash-funded** — read it as one-time repair, not organic deleveraging. Management leads with an ex-lease ~0.73–1.43x basis; the conservative like-for-like figure is 2.57x.
- **Maturity wall and refinancing:** no bullet. Flat ~₹1.0–1.3 Bn/yr secured term ladder + long self-amortising lease tail; dated term maturity inside 12 months ~₹1.3 Bn vs ₹5.36 Bn cash (~4x). Refinancing is **secured/self-funded** for the term debt and the refi cost steps *down* (~−50 bps) at the 5.25% repo; the one exposed item is the roll of the ~₹2.8 Bn on-demand overdraft.
- **Liquidity runway:** ~15 months on maintenance capex; ~31 months / FCF-covered if the overdraft rolls; ~10.8 months as the conservative bound. It depends on (a) the secured working-capital line continuing to roll (availability undisclosed) and (b) ~₹917 Mn/yr of term amortisation being refinanced; ~70% of the runway is in-hand cash.
- **Tightest covenant and headroom:** **undisclosed — Not assessable.** Only quantified anchor is management's self-imposed 2.5x ex-lease ceiling (~68% headroom). Bilateral bank loans almost certainly carry covenants this analysis cannot see — no early-warning trigger is visible.
- **Largest live off-balance-sheet / contingent exposure:** the Vizag (VHCRPL) put + forward acquisition obligations (~₹2.0 Bn recognised, ~₹3.3 Bn gross), of which ₹1,543 Mn already crystallised (paid Apr-2026); plus the ₹517.6 Mn EPCG export-obligation duty tail. Max contingent exposure ~50.5% of equity (a floor — three items unquantified).
- **Stress break point:** the first thing to break is an **assumed 2.0x lease-inclusive coverage covenant at ~−24% EBITDA** (a labeled assumption — no covenant is disclosed). Liquidity barely breaks on earnings alone (~−85% with the overdraft rolled); the next real cliff is the **on-demand overdraft being called** (a counterparty action), which opens a ~₹0.6–1.3 Bn 12-month gap in a −40%/−60% downside. A −30%/−40% recession is survivable on liquidity with no equity or asset sale required.
- **Partial-data caps applied:** no maturity schedule → solvency strength max 70; no covenant disclosure → covenant headroom Not assessable + usefulness max 75; no revolver availability → liquidity runway max 60; off-balance-sheet partly undisclosed → solvency strength max 75 (superseded by the 70 cap). The stress test ran in full (EBITDA base present) — downside resilience IS assessable.
- **Biggest missing data point (single highest-value next data request):** the **lender credit agreement / loan-facility terms sheet** — it would deliver covenant thresholds, the year-by-year maturity schedule, revolver/cash-credit availability, and change-of-control/cross-default triggers in one source, lifting three of the four binding caps at once.
- **Correction to carry:** the triage said "no credit rating in pool"; in fact **ICRA A+ (Stable) / A1+** is disclosed in the FY24-25 AR (three agents cite it). Investment-grade-equivalent domestic rating exists — note it, do not infer it; a full rating report is still absent.
- **Explicit handoff:** the master synthesizer's **"Balance Sheet and Survival Test" section should defer to this synthesis.** The stress break points here (assumed-covenant trip ~−24%; overdraft-call gap ~₹0.6–1.3 Bn at −40%/−60%; liquidity essentially unbreakable on earnings to ~−85% rolled) are the inputs for the master's downside scenario and risk register. **This module assigns no probabilities, no rating, no fair value, and no position size** — the master owns those.

#### 8. Simple Summary

- **Debt:** ₹9,104 Mn of borrowings (₹17,354 Mn including leases); net of ₹5,360 Mn cash, net debt is ₹3,744 Mn ex-lease / ₹11,993 Mn incl. leases — leverage 0.80x ex-lease, **2.57x including leases**. ~96% floating, largely unhedged, ~8% cost.
- **Maturity wall:** no bullet — a flat ~₹1.0–1.3 Bn/yr term ladder plus a long self-amortising lease tail. The ~₹1.3 Bn of dated term debt due within a year is covered ~4x by cash; **it is covered.** The one open item is rolling the ~₹2.8 Bn on-demand overdraft.
- **Liquidity runway:** ~15 months on maintenance capex; ~31 months / cash-flow-covered if the overdraft rolls.
- **Tightest covenant:** **unknown — no lender covenant is disclosed.** The only yardstick is management's self-imposed 2.5x leverage limit, which it sits ~68% below.
- **Biggest off-balance-sheet exposure:** the Vizag acquisition put/forward obligations (~₹2.0 Bn recognised, ~₹3.3 Bn gross; ₹1,543 Mn already paid Apr-2026), plus a ₹517.6 Mn EPCG import-duty tail.
- **Survives a 30–60% EBITDA drop?** **On cash, yes** — even a −60% year leaves ~14 months of runway if the overdraft rolls. It first "breaks" on an *assumed* coverage covenant at ~−24% (needs a waiver, not new money); the real risk is the overdraft being pulled.
- **Rating / key data:** **ICRA A+ (Stable) / A1+** disclosed in the FY24-25 AR (investment-grade-equivalent, domestic). Key gaps: lender covenants, year-by-year maturity table, committed-facility availability, full rating report, FY26 contingencies note.
- **Useful for the master synthesizer?** **Yes (usefulness 72/100).** Leverage, the wall, runway, contingencies, and the stress break points are all quantified; the cap on usefulness reflects the missing covenants and facility-availability data, not weak analysis.

---

## BSS-00 · solvency-data-triage — Solvency Data Triage — HCG

### Solvency Data Triage — HCG

Company: HealthCare Global Enterprises Limited (NSE: HCG / BSE: 539787). Cancer-care and multispecialty hospital network operator, India (plus Kenya). Reporting currency: INR (Ind AS). Business type: **operating company** with corporate term-loan debt and Ind AS 116 lease liabilities. **Not** a bank or insurer — the Business Type Applicability Gate does **not** trigger; the debt/EBITDA framework applies.

#### 5. Partial-Data Flags

| Missing Data | Applies? (Y/N) | Affected Agents | Cap Applied |
|---|---|---|---|
| No debt maturity schedule | Partial (Y) — qualitative repayment terms only, no clean year-by-year wall | 02, 06 | refi-risk confidence Low; solvency max 70 |
| No covenant disclosure | Y | 04, 06 | covenant headroom not assessable; usefulness max 75 |
| No cash flow statement | N — present (FY24-25 AR + CapIQ) | 03, 04, 06 | — (not triggered) |
| No undrawn-facility disclosure | Y | 03 | liquidity = cash only |
| No interest-expense detail | N — finance costs + rate terms present | 04 | — (not triggered) |
| No EBITDA base | N — FY26 and multi-year EBITDA present | 06 | — (not triggered) |

Additional caps from MODULE_RULES.md that will bind:
- **No credit ratings** → agent 99 notes the absence; do not infer a rating.
- **No revolver availability / borrowing-base detail** → liquidity cannot include the cash-credit/working-capital line; treat as "availability unknown"; **Liquidity runway max 60**.
- **No covenant-EBITDA definition / addback detail** → headroom quality cannot be judged; cap covenant confidence; **Covenant headroom max 60** (and "Not assessable" since no threshold at all).
- HoldCo/OpCo placement only partially mapped → if material debt sits at a non-upstreaming entity, **Solvency strength max 70** until resolved.

#### 6. Sufficiency Verdict

- **Verdict:** Partial
- **Reason:** A recent audited balance sheet, the borrowings note (amounts, security, rates, repayment tenor), and full cash-flow statements are all present, so leverage, liquidity, coverage, and a stress test can all be built — but covenant thresholds, a clean year-by-year maturity table, undrawn-committed-facility availability, and credit ratings are absent, capping refinancing, covenant, and liquidity confidence.
- **Sections that can run:** capital structure & leverage (full); maturity wall (built from current/non-current split and qualitative repayment terms, flagged); liquidity runway (cash + FCF; revolver excluded as availability-unknown); coverage (full — finance costs and rates available); covenants (assessment LABELED as assumed market covenants — true headroom not computable); contingencies (full — Note 33, guarantees, capital commitments); downside stress test (full — EBITDA base available; calibrate against earnings/03_margin-drivers and use cash-backed EBITDA per earnings/06_earnings-quality).
- **Active partial-data caps:**
  - Refinancing-risk confidence Low; Solvency strength max 70 (no clean maturity schedule).
  - Covenant headroom = "Not assessable"; Overall usefulness max 75 (no covenant disclosure); Covenant headroom max 60.
  - Liquidity = cash + FCF only; Liquidity runway max 60 (no undrawn-facility / revolver-availability disclosure).
  - No credit ratings — note absence, do not infer.
- **Critical missing items:**
  - Year-by-year debt maturity schedule (the precise wall).
  - Financial-covenant thresholds and covenant-EBITDA definition (leverage / coverage / DSCR limits and addbacks).
  - Committed undrawn facility / cash-credit availability and borrowing-base detail.
  - Credit rating report(s).
  - Change-of-control / cross-default / rating-trigger terms.
- **Single highest-value missing document:** the credit agreement / loan-facility terms sheet (would deliver covenant thresholds, the maturity schedule, revolver availability, and change-of-control/cross-default triggers in one source).

---

## BSS-01 · capital-structure-and-leverage — Capital Structure & Leverage — HCG

### Capital Structure & Leverage — HCG

HealthCare Global Enterprises Limited (NSE: HCG / BSE: 539787). Report date: 2026-06-01. Cancer-care and multispecialty hospital network operator (India + one centre in Kenya). **Reporting currency: INR million (₹ Mn), consolidated, Ind AS**, unless stated. Fiscal year ends 31 March (FY26 = year ended 31 Mar 2026).

**Business-type note:** operating company with corporate term-loan debt plus Ind AS 116 lease liabilities. Not a bank/insurer — the debt/EBITDA framework applies [00_solvency-data-triage; FY24-25 AR, Note 18 (Borrowings)].

**EBITDA basis used throughout this report:** HCG discloses two constructs, kept separate. **Reported EBITDA** (audited statutory operating result, pre other-income) FY26 = ₹4,657.9 Mn; **Adjusted EBITDA** (company-defined: Reported + ESOP cost + one-time costs) FY26 = ₹4,711.3 Mn [FY26 results deck, p.19]. Leverage is shown on both. EBITDA is post-Ind AS 116 (leases capitalised), so lease-inclusive leverage is the like-for-like view; an ex-lease (pre-Ind AS) view is also shown because the company quotes its headline leverage that way.

**Source note:** the latest *audited statutory* financial statements in the pool are FY24-25 (year ended 31 Mar 2025), which carry the full borrowings note (Note 18) with security, rates and tenor. FY26 (year ended 31 Mar 2026) balance-sheet and net-debt figures come from the **board-approved audited results presentation dated 19 May 2026** and the **20 May 2026 earnings call** — the FY26 full statutory borrowings note is not yet in the pool, so the FY26 instrument-level seniority/security split is carried forward from the FY25 note (the facilities are the same instruments, one year on).

Cross-module inputs read: `earnings/01_historical-financials.md` (EBITDA, net debt, leverage trend), `business-model/11_capital-allocation-governance.md` (debt trajectory and drivers). Valuation cross-module input (`valuation/01_price-and-capital-structure.md`) not available — valuation module not yet run; proceeding on this module's own read of the data pool.

---

#### 1. Debt Stack

Figures as at 31 March 2026 (latest), consolidated, ₹ Mn. Borrowings figures are the **balance-sheet (gross) line** (₹9,104.1 Mn = NC 5,991.9 + Current 3,112.2) [FY26 results deck, Consolidated Balance Sheet, p.~28]. Instrument-level breakdown (amounts, security, seniority, rates, tenor) is from the **FY25 statutory borrowings note** (Note 18), which is the latest full instrument disclosure in the pool; FY25 instrument amounts are shown where the FY26 split is not separately disclosed, and flagged as such.

| Instrument | Amount (₹ Mn) | Entity (HoldCo/OpCo) | Secured? | Seniority | Collateral | Maturity | Rate (fixed/floating) | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Short-term debt — bank overdraft / working-capital (repayable on demand) | 2,829.34 (FY25); incl. in current borrowings FY26 | Parent + LLPs/subsidiaries (OpCo-level) | Secured | Senior secured; 1st pari-passu on current assets, 2nd pari-passu on fixed assets | Current assets (1st), fixed assets (2nd) | On demand | Floating: Repo +1.70–2.00%, MIBOR +1.84%, 1-yr T-bill +1.52–1.65% | FY24-25 AR, Note 18 / Note 18.1.4 |
| Current portion of long-term debt (incl. in current borrowings) | 916.78 term loans + 155.35 deferred + 75.00 other (FY25) | Parent + subsidiaries/LLPs | Mostly secured | Senior secured (term loans); unsecured (deferred/other) | Per facility (see below) | Within 12 months | Floating (term loans); 3–10% (deferred) | FY24-25 AR, Note 18 |
| Term loans from banks — secured (total gross) | 6,791.84 (FY25) | Parent, HCG Medi-Surge, HCG Oncology LLP, Suchirayu, Nagpur (NCHRI), Cancer Care Kenya, others | Secured | Senior secured | 1st/2nd pari-passu charges on movable + immovable fixed assets and current assets; some exclusive equipment charges; one facility 100% NCGTC credit-guaranteed | Installments over 6–12 yrs (incl. 1–3 yr moratoria); Kenya 6 yrs | Floating: Repo +1.75% to +3.25%; 1-yr T-bill +1.34–1.77%; MCLR +0.20–0.25%; Kenya = bank's variable KES base rate | FY24-25 AR, Note 18.1.1 (Facilities 1–9) |
| Deferred payment liabilities — unsecured | 132.81 NC + 155.35 Cur = 288.16 (FY25) | Parent/OpCo (equipment vendors) | Unsecured | Unsecured | None | Installments over 3–7 yrs | 3% to 10% p.a. | FY24-25 AR, Note 18.1.3 |
| Loans from other parties — unsecured | 37.97 NC + 75.00 Cur = 112.97 (FY25) | OpCo | Unsecured | Unsecured | None | Interest-free (one tranche, as funds available); one at 10% repayable from Apr 2026 | 0% / 10% p.a. | FY24-25 AR, Note 18.1.2 |
| Finance / capital leases (Ind AS 116 lease liabilities) | 8,249.5 (FY26: NC 7,599.8 + Cur 649.7) | OpCo (hospital premises, equipment) | n/a (lease) | Lease obligation | Leased assets (right-of-use) | Lease terms (multi-year) | Implicit ~10% incremental borrowing rate | FY26 deck, Balance Sheet p.~28; FY24-25 AR, Note 5.1 (capitalisation rate 10%) |
| **Total interest-bearing borrowings (ex-lease), FY26** | **9,104.1** | — | Predominantly secured (~96% at FY25) | Mostly senior secured | — | — | ~96% floating; avg cost ~8% | FY26 deck, Balance Sheet p.~28; transcript 20 May 2026 (8%) |
| **Total gross debt incl. leases, FY26** | **17,353.6** | — | — | — | — | — | — | Derived (9,104.1 + 8,249.5) |

**Currency of debt:** substantially all INR-denominated. At 31 Mar 2025, only ₹214.34 Mn was non-INR (USD/EUR equipment-vendor loans + Kenya working-capital/term loan) [FY24-25 AR, MD&A, p.173]. FX exposure on debt is immaterial.

**Secured vs unsecured (FY25 consolidated, the latest instrument-level split):** total secured ₹9,621.18 Mn (term loans 6,791.84 + bank overdraft 2,829.34); total unsecured ₹401.13 Mn (deferred payment 288.16 + other 112.97); total ₹10,022.31 Mn [FY24-25 AR, MD&A "Liabilities – borrowings", p.173]. **~96% of borrowings are secured and senior** — there is no subordinated or junior tranche, and no bonds/notes (HCG has no listed debt; "no External Commercial Borrowing by the Company" [FY24-25 AR, p.~93]).

**Rate type:** virtually all term loans and the working-capital line are floating (linked to repo rate, 1-yr T-bill, MIBOR or MCLR plus a spread) [FY24-25 AR, Note 18.1.1–18.1.4]. Management states the **average interest cost is ~8%** [Q4/FY26 transcript, Q&A, 20 May 2026]. Interest-rate-swap/hedge disclosure was not located in the pool — floating exposure appears largely unhedged (flagged for agent 02/04/06).

**Tie-out to balance sheet:** FY26 borrowings line = NC 5,991.9 + Current 3,112.2 = **₹9,104.1 Mn**; FY25 = NC 6,045.84 + Current 3,976.47 = **₹10,022.31 Mn**, which ties exactly to the FY25 statutory consolidated balance sheet and MD&A total [FY24-25 AR, Consolidated Balance Sheet, p.312; MD&A p.173]. ✓

#### 2. Other Debt-Like Obligations

| Obligation | Amount (₹ Mn) | Treatment | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|
| Operating / finance leases (Ind AS 116 lease liabilities) | 8,249.5 (FY26); 8,349.9 (FY25) | **On balance sheet** — Ind AS 116 capitalises leases (India = IFRS-equivalent). Already included in gross debt incl. leases above. No separate off-balance-sheet operating-lease pool. | FY26 deck, Balance Sheet p.~28; FY24-25 AR, Consolidated Balance Sheet, p.312 |
| Pension / gratuity (defined-benefit) underfunding | 257.71 (FY25); net liability NC 191.21 + Cur 66.50 | Net DB liability on balance sheet (DBO 273.81 − plan assets 16.10). Essentially unfunded but small (~0.06x EBITDA). Net interest runs through finance costs. | FY24-25 AR, Note 37 (consolidated), p.~374 |
| Vizag (VHCRPL) put option — minority's right to sell residual stake | 634.00 (at 31 Mar 2025; ₹608 Mn at inception Oct-2024) | Recognised as other current/non-current financial liability (not in borrowings). Debt-like: a future cash call to buy out a minority. | FY24-25 AR, Note 45.1, p.374; MD&A p.173 (put options ₹1,401.34 Mn total) |
| Vizag forward liability — obligation to acquire additional 34% | 1,347.00 (at acquisition) | Recognised financial liability (not in borrowings). Debt-like deferred acquisition obligation. | FY24-25 AR, Note 45.1, p.374 |
| Vizag deferred (2nd-tranche) acquisition consideration — **paid Apr 2026** | 1,543 (cash outflow Apr-2026, post-FY26 balance sheet) | Post-balance-sheet cash call. The company adds it back to net debt to show a ₹4,930 Mn "adjusted" net-debt level. Treat as a near-term claim on liquidity. | FY26 deck, "Capital Expenditure and Net Debt", p.~14 |
| Preferred equity | None | HCG has no preferred shares; equity is a single ordinary class (₹10 face). | FY24-25 AR, Note 15 (share capital) |

Note: the put-option and forward/deferred-acquisition liabilities are **not** interest-bearing borrowings and are excluded from the gross-debt total in Section 1, but they are real future cash claims (~₹2.0–2.2 Bn aggregate) and matter to liquidity/coverage (handed to agents 03/05). The ₹1,543 Mn Vizag tranche has already been paid in Apr 2026.

The strict balance-sheet "cash & cash equivalents" line is ₹5,360.5 Mn (FY26). **Restricted/trapped cash flag:** ~₹287 Mn margin money is restricted and is *not* freely available liquidity — it is flagged here rather than silently netted. No disclosure of offshore/trapped cash beyond the small Kenya operation; not quantified in the pool.

#### 4. Gross & Net Debt

Definition used: **net debt = total interest-bearing borrowings − cash & equivalents** (CLAUDE.md default). Both an ex-lease view and a lease-inclusive view are shown because (a) Ind AS 116 puts leases on the balance sheet and (b) the company quotes its headline leverage on an ex-lease ("pre-Ind AS") basis. The company's own "net debt" further nets margin-money out of borrowings and counts mutual funds in cash, producing the lowest line; all three are shown to avoid hiding the gap.

| − Cash & cash equivalents (balance-sheet line) | (5,360.5) | FY26 deck, Balance Sheet p.~28 |
| **Net debt, ex-lease (cash only)** | **3,743.6** | Derived |
| **Net debt, incl. lease (cash only)** | **11,993.1** | Derived (ties to earnings module 11,993) |
| Memo: company-defined net debt, ex-lease (borrowings net of ~₹287 Mn margin money 8,817 − cash incl. MF 5,430) | 3,387 | FY26 deck, net-debt note p.~14 |
