#### 5. Leverage Ratios

Computed on FY26 (year ended 31 Mar 2026). EBITDA base: **Reported EBITDA ₹4,657.9 Mn** and **Adjusted EBITDA ₹4,711.3 Mn** [FY26 results deck, p.19]. Both EBITDA figures are post-Ind AS 116, so the lease-inclusive leverage lines are the correct like-for-like comparison; the ex-lease lines are shown to match the company's own headline presentation. Formula: leverage = debt ÷ EBITDA.

| Ratio | On Reported EBITDA (4,657.9) | On Adjusted EBITDA (4,711.3) | Source / basis |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| **Gross debt incl. leases / EBITDA** | **3.73x** | 3.68x | Gross debt 17,353.6 |
| Gross borrowings ex-lease / EBITDA | 1.95x | 1.93x | Gross borrowings 9,104.1 |
| **Net debt incl. leases / EBITDA** (cash only) | **2.57x** | 2.55x | Net debt incl. lease 11,993.1 |
| Net debt ex-lease / EBITDA (cash only) | 0.80x | 0.79x | Net debt ex-lease 3,743.6 |
| Memo: company-defined net debt ex-lease / EBITDA | 0.73x | 0.72x | Co-defined ND 3,387 (deck shows ~0.73x reported) |
| Memo: company-defined net debt adjusted for Vizag / EBITDA | 1.06x | 1.05x | ND+Vizag 4,930 |
| **Debt / capital — incl. leases** | **55.2%** | (n/a) | Debt incl. lease 17,353.6 ÷ (debt + equity 14,102.1) |
| Debt / capital — ex-lease | 39.2% | (n/a) | Borrowings 9,104.1 ÷ (borrowings + equity) |
| **Debt / equity — incl. leases** | **1.23x** | (n/a) | Debt incl. lease ÷ equity 14,102.1 |
| Debt / equity — ex-lease (borrowings only) | 0.65x | (n/a) | Borrowings ÷ equity |

**Reconciliation of the leverage gap (important).** Management's headline "net debt/EBITDA of 1.4x" [Q4/FY26 transcript, 20 May 2026] and the deck's "~0.73x" / "1.43x" are **ex-lease, pre-Ind AS** figures that also (i) net ~₹287 Mn margin money out of borrowings, (ii) count mutual funds in cash, and (iii) the 1.43x version *adds back* the ₹1,543 Mn Vizag tranche paid in Apr-2026 against a CFO-based denominator. On the same Ind AS basis as the EBITDA being earned, **net debt including capitalised leases is 2.57x reported EBITDA** — about 1.1x–1.8x higher than the figure management leads with. Both views are legitimate; the lease-inclusive figure is the conservative, like-for-like one and is the anchor downstream agents should stress.

**Interest-coverage preview (handed to agent 04 for full work):** FY26 EBITDA/finance cost = 4,657.9 ÷ 1,765.7 = **2.64x**; EBIT/finance cost = 2,466.1 ÷ 1,765.7 = **1.40x** [FY26 deck, p.19]. Finance cost includes ~₹697 Mn of Ind AS 116 lease interest (FY25 basis), so ex-lease cash interest coverage is materially higher; agent 04 owns the full coverage decomposition.

---

#### 6. Leverage Trend

| Metric (₹ Mn) | FY24 | FY25 | FY26 | Direction |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| Gross borrowings (ex-lease) | 6,727.6 | 10,022.3 | 9,104.1 | Rose then fell |
| Gross debt incl. leases | 12,315.7 | 18,372.2 | 17,353.6 | Rose then eased |
| Net debt incl. leases (cash only) | 10,018ᵃ | 16,013.5 | 11,993.1 | Up sharply then down |
| Reported EBITDA | 3,296 | 3,873 | 4,657.9 | Rising |
| **Net debt incl. leases / Reported EBITDA** | ~3.0xᵃ | 4.13x | 2.57x | Down in FY26 |
| Net debt ex-lease / Reported EBITDA (cash only) | ~1.2xᵃ | 1.98x | 0.80x | Down in FY26 |
| Memo: company-defined net debt ex-lease / EBITDA | n/a | 1.63x (1.0xᵇ deck) | 0.73x | Falling |

ᵃ FY24 net-debt-incl-lease ₹10,018 Mn and gross-borrowings ₹6,727.6 Mn are from the earnings module / FY24-25 AR comparative; FY24 EBITDA-leverage ratios are approximate (derived). ᵇ The deck shows FY25 ex-lease company-defined net debt/EBITDA on a "pre-Ind AS" basis.

**Direction and driver (2–3 sentences).** Leverage **rose materially into FY25, then fell in FY26.** The FY24→FY25 build was acquisition- and capex-driven: net debt incl. leases jumped from ~₹10.0 Bn to ₹16.0 Bn as HCG funded the Vizag (VHCRPL) acquisition (₹3,410 Mn consideration, ₹1,972 Mn net cash) and a step-up in growth capex (PP&E additions ₹2,089 Mn FY25), pushing net debt incl. leases to 4.13x reported EBITDA [FY24-25 AR, Note 45.1; business-model/11; earnings/01]. The FY26 fall to 2.57x (incl. leases) was driven **predominantly by a ₹4,250 Mn rights issue completed in Q4 FY26 (oversubscribed 1.3x) plus some long-term-debt repayment and EBITDA growth (+20% reported)** — *not* by free cash generation (FY26 FCF was only ~₹586 Mn as capex climbed to ₹2,885 Mn) [Q4/FY26 transcript, prepared remarks; FY26 deck p.14-16; earnings/01]. Under new control (KKR took sole control 30 May 2025), management states an **internal net-debt/EBITDA ceiling of 2.5x** (ex-lease basis) and cut headline leverage from "2.2x–2.3x to 1.4x" [Q4/FY26 transcript, Q&A; business-model/11].

---

#### 6A. HoldCo / OpCo & Structural Subordination

| Item | Evidence | Why It Matters |
|---|---|---|
| Where debt sits (HoldCo vs OpCo) | Borrowings are spread across the listed parent (HealthCare Global Enterprises Ltd) **and** operating subsidiaries/LLPs (HCG Medi-Surge, HCG Oncology LLP, Suchirayu, Nagpur/NCHRI, Vizag/VHCRPL, Cancer Care Kenya). Term-loan security is granted at the operating-entity level (charges on those entities' assets), and the parent has given **corporate guarantees** for subsidiaries' term loans (₹631.57 Mn at 31 Mar 2025, plus a separate guarantee for bank-guarantee/cash-credit facilities) [FY24-25 AR, Note 46(c), p.~300; Note 18.1.1]. | HCG is an operating-holding company, not a pure HoldCo with isolated debt-at-top. Debt sits close to the assets that secure it; structural subordination of parent creditors is **limited** because lenders hold OpCo-level charges and parent guarantees rather than relying on dividend upstreaming. |
| Upstreaming constraints (dividend blockers, regulatory) | No dividend has ever been paid; consolidated retained earnings are **negative** (₹(4,092) Mn at 31 Mar 2025), which legally precludes dividends [business-model/11; FY24-25 AR, Note 16]. Subsidiary-to-parent cash movement is via inter-corporate loans/guarantees, several of which are impaired (provision for non-recoverability ₹181.63 Mn) [FY24-25 AR, Note 46(b)]. | Cash is retained in the group for capex/M&A; there is no dividend leakage. The negative retained earnings and impaired intra-group loans show some subsidiaries do not generate upstreamable surplus — relevant to where coverage actually sits (agent 04). |
| Material restricted / trapped cash | ~₹287 Mn margin money restricted (FY26); small Kenya operation (foreign) not separately quantified for cash-trapping in the pool [FY26 deck footnote; 00_triage]. | Restricted cash modestly overstates usable liquidity; quantified and flagged, not netted silently. |

**Conclusion:** Not a classic structurally-subordinated HoldCo/OpCo problem — debt is OpCo-level and secured, with parent guarantees, and there is no HoldCo-level bond stack relying on dividends. Structural subordination risk is therefore low-to-moderate, but the **negative consolidated retained earnings and impaired intra-group loans** are noted for downstream coverage/liquidity work. The triage's "HoldCo material debt but upstreaming unclear" cap (solvency strength max 70) is **not** fully triggered here because debt placement maps cleanly to operating entities; the cap that does bind is the no-maturity-schedule one (see Anchor caveats).

---

#### 7. Leverage Anchor Summary (canonical numbers for downstream agents)

Use these figures verbatim. **Reporting currency: INR million (₹ Mn), consolidated, Ind AS, as at 31 March 2026** unless a date is stated.

- **Gross debt (interest-bearing borrowings, ex-lease):** ₹9,104.1 Mn
- **Lease liabilities (Ind AS 116):** ₹8,249.5 Mn
- **Gross debt incl. leases:** ₹17,353.6 Mn
- **Cash & cash equivalents (balance-sheet line):** ₹5,360.5 Mn (of which ~₹21 Mn mutual funds; a further ~₹287 Mn margin money is restricted and held outside this line, in "bank balances other than cash")
- **Net debt, ex-lease (cash only):** ₹3,743.6 Mn
- **Net debt, incl. leases (cash only):** ₹11,993.1 Mn
- **EBITDA base used:** Reported EBITDA ₹4,657.9 Mn (FY26); Adjusted EBITDA ₹4,711.3 Mn (FY26). Reported is the default; adjusted differs by only +₹53 Mn (ESOP + one-time).
- **Net debt / EBITDA — incl. leases (conservative, like-for-like): 2.57x reported / 2.55x adjusted**
- **Net debt / EBITDA — ex-lease (cash only): 0.80x reported / 0.79x adjusted**
- **Company-headline net debt / EBITDA (ex-lease, margin-money-netted, MF in cash): ~0.73x; "1.4x" / "1.43x" when adjusted for the ₹1,543 Mn Vizag tranche paid Apr-2026** [Q4/FY26 transcript; FY26 deck p.14-16]
- **Gross debt incl. leases / EBITDA: 3.73x reported**
- **Debt/capital (incl. leases): 55.2%; Debt/equity (incl. leases): 1.23x** (equity ₹14,102.1 Mn)
- **Average interest cost:** ~8% (management); ~96% of borrowings floating, FX exposure immaterial (~₹214 Mn non-INR)
- **Credit rating:** ICRA **A+ (Stable)** long-term, **A1+** short-term; no revision during FY25 [FY24-25 AR, Corporate Governance Report, "O. Credit Ratings", p.~155; MD&A p.174]. (Pool contains no full rating-agency report — note the rating but treat detail as limited.)

**Caveats downstream agents must propagate:**
1. **Two leverage bases.** Management leads with ex-lease, margin-money-netted leverage (~0.7x–1.4x). On the same Ind AS basis as the EBITDA earned, **net debt incl. leases is 2.57x**. Stress and covenant work should use the lease-inclusive figure as the conservative anchor and state which basis is in play.
2. **FY26 deleveraging is equity-funded, not cash-funded.** The drop from 4.13x (FY25, incl. leases) to 2.57x was driven by a ₹4,250 Mn rights issue, not free cash flow (FY26 FCF ~₹586 Mn). It is a one-time balance-sheet repair, not organic deleveraging [earnings/01].
3. **Near-term cash claims outside borrowings.** The ₹1,543 Mn Vizag deferred consideration (paid Apr-2026) and the residual Vizag put/forward liabilities (~₹0.6–1.3 Bn) are real liquidity claims not captured in gross debt — hand to agents 03/05.
4. **No clean year-by-year maturity schedule** in the pool (only current/non-current split + qualitative tenor) → per triage, refinancing-risk confidence Low and solvency-strength max 70 bind at the synthesis layer. Maturity granularity is agent 02's problem.
5. **~96% floating-rate, largely unhedged** at ~8% average cost — a rate shock flows almost fully through to interest (agent 04/06).
6. EBITDA figures are post-Ind AS 116; the small adjusted-vs-reported gap (+₹53 Mn) means using reported EBITDA is conservative and adequate.

No solvency verdict is rendered here — that is the synthesizer's role (agent 99).

---

## BSS-02 · maturity-wall-and-refinancing — Maturity Wall & Refinancing — HCG

### Maturity Wall & Refinancing — HCG

HealthCare Global Enterprises Limited (NSE: HCG / BSE: 539787). Report date: 2026-06-01. Cancer-care and multispecialty hospital operator (India + one centre in Kenya). **Reporting currency: INR million (₹ Mn), consolidated, Ind AS.** Fiscal year ends 31 March (FY26 = year ended 31 Mar 2026). Figures are anchored to module `01_capital-structure-and-leverage.md`.

**Source / data-vintage note (read first).** Module 01's canonical gross-debt anchor is **FY26 (31 Mar 2026): borrowings ₹9,104.1 Mn + Ind AS 116 leases ₹8,249.5 Mn = ₹17,353.6 Mn**. The only **year-by-year** maturity disclosure in the pool is the consolidated liquidity-risk contractual-maturity table **as at 31 March 2025** (FY25) [FY24-25 AR, Note 40 (Financial risk management), p.368]. The FY26 statutory borrowings note (with a refreshed year-by-year table) is **not yet in the pool**. So this report does two things: (a) anchors the next-12-month figure to the **FY26 current/non-current split** (the firm data), and (b) characterises the **beyond-12-month shape** from the **FY25** undiscounted schedule, one year stale, clearly labelled. Per the module partial-data rule and the triage, **refinancing-risk confidence is capped at Low** because the granular schedule at the canonical (FY26) date is absent [00_solvency-data-triage, §5; MODULE_RULES partial-data table].

**Cross-module inputs read:** `01_capital-structure-and-leverage.md` (gross debt, instruments, rates); `earnings/01_historical-financials.md` (CFO/FCF/capex). `business-model/11_capital-allocation-governance.md` reflected via module 01. Valuation cross-module input not available — valuation module not yet run; proceeding on this module's own read.

---

#### 1. Maturity Schedule

Reporting currency: **₹ Mn, consolidated, Ind AS.** Two views are shown because the firm data and the year-by-year shape come from different dates.

##### 1A. FY26 anchor (31 Mar 2026) — carrying value, current/non-current only (the firm next-12m figure)

| Period | Amount Due (₹ Mn) | % of Total Debt | Instrument(s) | Source |
|---|---:|---:|---|---|
| Within 12 months — **borrowings** (current) | 3,112.2 | 34.2% of ex-lease borrowings | Bank overdraft (on-demand) ~₹1,783.3 + current portion of secured term loans + deferred-payment instalments (~₹1,329) | FY26 deck, Consolidated Balance Sheet p.~28; CF reconciliation (overdraft 1,783.3) |
| Within 12 months — **leases** (current) | 649.7 | 7.9% of lease balance | Ind AS 116 lease liabilities (current) | FY26 deck, Balance Sheet p.~28 |
| Beyond 12 months — **borrowings** (non-current) | 5,991.9 | 65.8% of ex-lease borrowings | Secured bank term loans (4–10 yr tenors), unsecured deferred-payment / other | FY26 deck, Balance Sheet p.~28 |
| Beyond 12 months — **leases** (non-current) | 7,599.8 | 92.1% of lease balance | Ind AS 116 lease liabilities (non-current) | FY26 deck, Balance Sheet p.~28 |
| **Total ex-lease borrowings (carrying)** | **9,104.1** | 100% | — | Ties to module 01 ✓ |
| **Total incl. leases (carrying)** | **17,353.6** | — | — | Ties to module 01 ✓ |

**Reconciling item:** the FY26 view has no year-by-year split beyond current/non-current — that is the missing data. Of the ₹3,112.2 Mn "within 12 months," **~₹1,783.3 Mn (57%) is the on-demand bank overdraft** — a working-capital line that is contractually repayable on demand but functions as a rolled facility, not a hard-dated bullet [FY26 deck, CF reconciliation; FY24-25 AR, Note 18.1.2]. The genuinely dated term-debt/deferred-payment maturity inside 12 months is **~₹1,329 Mn**.

##### 1B. FY25 year-by-year shape (31 Mar 2025) — undiscounted contractual maturities (the only granular table; one year stale)

This is on an **undiscounted** basis (includes future interest; for variable-rate loans the principal is per the repayment schedule), so totals exceed carrying value [FY24-25 AR, Note 40, p.368].

| Period | Borrowings (₹ Mn) | % of undisc. borrowings | Leases (₹ Mn) | Source |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| Within 12 months (< 1 yr) | 4,020.81 | 35.6% | 1,341.86 | FY24-25 AR, Note 40, p.368 |
| Year 2 (1–2 yr) | 1,254.64 | 11.1% | 1,205.97 | same |
| Year 3 (2–3 yr) | 1,239.37 | 11.0% | 1,162.75 | same |
| Year 4 (3–4 yr) | 1,059.05 | 9.4% | 1,121.48 | same |
| Year 5+ (> 4 yr) | 3,721.02 | 32.9% | 9,477.86 | same |
| **Total (undiscounted)** | **11,294.89** | 100% | **14,309.92** | same |
| Memo: carrying value | 10,022.31 | — | 8,349.9 | FY24-25 AR, Balance Sheet p.312 |

Undiscounted/carrying ratio for borrowings = 1.13x (the ~13% gap is future interest). The FY25 < 1-yr borrowings bucket (₹4,020.81 Mn) likewise includes the on-demand overdraft (FY25 ₹2,797.78 Mn ≈ 70% of that bucket); the dated term/interest portion inside 12 months was only ~₹1,223 Mn [FY24-25 AR, Note 18 / Note 40].

**Read on the two views:** between FY25 and FY26 the overdraft fell from ₹2,797.8 Mn to ₹1,783.3 Mn (−₹1,014 Mn) and total borrowings fell from ₹10,022.3 Mn to ₹9,104.1 Mn, funded by the Q4-FY26 rights issue and some term-loan repayment [FY26 deck p.14-16; transcript 20 May 2026]. The FY25 year-by-year shape therefore overstates the current absolute wall slightly but is the best available guide to the *profile* (front-and-back-loaded: ~⅓ in <1yr dominated by the rollable overdraft, then a thin ~10%/yr term-loan ladder for years 2–4, then a heavy lease-dominated tail beyond year 4).

---

#### 2. Maturity Profile Metrics

Computed on the FY25 undiscounted borrowings schedule (the only year-by-year data), with the FY26 carrying anchor shown alongside for the 12-month figure. Bucket-midpoint convention for WAM: 0.5 / 1.5 / 2.5 / 3.5 yr; ">4yr" midpoint = 7 yr (term tenors run 8–10 yr) — a 6-yr midpoint is shown as sensitivity.

| Metric | Value |
|---|---:|
| Weighted-average maturity — borrowings, all-in (incl. on-demand overdraft in <1yr) | ~3.3 yr (2.9 yr if >4yr midpoint = 6 yr) |
| Weighted-average maturity — **term debt only** (overdraft excluded as perpetually rolled) | ~4.2 yr |
| % due within 12 months — FY26 carrying (ex-lease borrowings) | **34.2%** (₹3,112.2 / 9,104.1); **but ~57% of that is the on-demand overdraft — dated term maturity is ~14.6%** (₹1,329 / 9,104.1) |
| % due within 12 months — FY25 undiscounted borrowings | 35.6% (₹4,020.81 / 11,294.89) |
| % due within 24 months — FY25 undiscounted borrowings | 46.7% |
| % due within 36 months — FY25 undiscounted borrowings | 57.7% |
| % due within 12 months — combined **incl. leases** (FY26 carrying) | **21.7%** (₹3,761.9 / 17,353.6) |
| Largest single maturity concentration | **The >4-yr tail**, dominated by **lease liabilities ₹9,477.86 Mn undiscounted** (66% of the lease book sits beyond year 4); within borrowings, the largest single bucket is the <1-yr bucket (₹4,020.81 Mn), which is ~70% rollable overdraft |

**Note on "largest single year":** the disclosure buckets everything beyond year 4 into one ">4 years" line, so a single peak year cannot be isolated from the pool — flagged as a data limitation. There is **no evidence of a single bullet-maturity spike** in years 1–4; the term-loan ladder is even at ~₹1.0–1.3 Bn per year (years 2–4). The concentration that does exist is the long-dated lease tail, which amortises over the lease terms rather than coming due as a refinanceable bullet.

---

#### 3. Rate Exposure

| Metric | Value | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Fixed-rate share | **~0–4%** (only the small unsecured deferred-payment/other loans at fixed 3–10% are non-floating; ~₹401 Mn of ₹10,022 Mn at FY25 ≈ 4%) | FY24-25 AR, Note 18.1.3–18.1.4; module 01 |
| Floating-rate share | **~96%** (all secured bank term loans + the bank overdraft, linked to repo / 1-yr T-bill / MIBOR / MCLR + spread) | FY24-25 AR, Note 18.1.1–18.1.2; module 01 |
| Weighted-average coupon (all-in) | **~8.0%** (management, May 2026) | Q4/FY26 transcript, Q&A, 20 May 2026 ("Our average interest cost is at 8%") |
| Term-loan benchmark spreads | Repo + 2.00–2.85%; 1-yr T-bill + 1.34–1.77%; MCLR + 0.20–0.25%; overdraft repo + 1.70–2.00% / MIBOR + 1.84% / 1-yr T-bill + 1.52–1.65% | FY24-25 AR, Note 18.1.1–18.1.2 |
| Current market refi rate (matching tenor) | **RBI repo 5.25%**; **364-day (1-yr) T-bill cut-off ~5.77%**; **1Y–2Y G-Sec ~6.30%**; 10-yr G-Sec ~7.0% | Web-sourced, indicative/unverified (see below) |
| Implied new-money term-loan rate today (benchmark + existing spreads) | **~7.1–8.1%** (repo-linked ~7.25–8.10%; T-bill-linked ~7.11–7.54%; blended ~7.5%) | Derived from spreads above + web benchmarks |
| **Estimated refi cost step-up** | **≈ −50 bps (a step-DOWN), indicative** | Derived; consistent with management guidance |

**Web-sourced benchmarks (labelled).** RBI repo rate held at **5.25%** (neutral stance, FY2026-27 first MPC) [Web: tradingeconomics.com / RBI, 2026 (indicative, unverified)]. India **364-day T-bill cut-off ~5.769%** on 13 May 2026 [Web: CEIC, 2026-05-13 (indicative, unverified)]. **1Y–2Y G-Sec ~6.30% YTM** [Web: CCIL Tenorwise Indicative Yields, 2026-06-01 (indicative, unverified)]. 10-yr G-Sec ~7.0% late May 2026 [Web: worldgovernmentbonds.com / tradingeconomics.com, 2026-05-29 (indicative, unverified)].

**Why the step-up is negative.** HCG's debt is ~96% floating and **already reprices automatically** with these benchmarks — it is not a stack of fixed-rate bullets that must be refinanced at a new, higher coupon. Much of the term-loan book was struck when the repo rate was ~6.5% (FY24-25); the repo has since fallen to **5.25%**, so the floating cost mechanically declines, and a *new* term loan refinancing a maturing one would price at roughly the same spread on a **lower** benchmark. Management corroborates: after the rights issue "and… we have repaid some long-term debts, we do expect some… reduction in the interest cost" against the current ~8% [Q4/FY26 transcript, Q&A, 20 May 2026]. The risk direction is therefore **a rate increase, not refinancing at a higher coupon** — see §4.

**Rate sensitivity.** With ~96% floating, a rate move flows almost fully through to interest. The FY25 AR's own +1% sensitivity on the term-loan portion (₹6,791.84 Mn variable long-term borrowings) is **−₹67.92 Mn pre-tax** [FY24-25 AR, Note 40(b)(ii), p.369]. **Caveat:** that disclosed sensitivity covers only the ₹6,791.84 Mn term loans and **excludes the ~₹1,783–2,798 Mn floating overdraft**, so the true full-book +100 bps impact is larger — roughly **−₹85 to −₹90 Mn pre-tax** on ~₹8.6–8.9 Bn of floating borrowings (Inference, not from filings; the AR sensitivity understates by leaving the overdraft out). A +200 bps shock on the full floating book is therefore on the order of **−₹170 to −₹180 Mn** pre-tax, ~3.6–3.9% of FY26 reported EBITDA (handed to agents 04/06). No interest-rate-swap/hedge disclosure was located in the pool — floating exposure appears largely **unhedged** [00_solvency-data-triage, §3].

---

#### 4. Refinancing Exposure

##### Refi Funding Plan (no speculation)

For the next-24-month maturities. The genuinely dated term-debt maturity is modest; the large "within 12m" figure is dominated by the rollable overdraft. Sources of repayment:

| Source of repayment for next-24m maturities | Amount (₹ Mn) | Evidence |
|---|---:|---|
| Cash on hand (balance-sheet line, 31 Mar 2026) | 5,360.5 (of which ~₹287 margin money restricted, held outside this line) | FY26 deck, Balance Sheet p.~28; module 01 §3 |
| Forecast FCF | **Not forecast here** — recent run-rate labelled: FY26 FCF (CFO − total capex) = **₹586 Mn**; FY26 CFO = ₹3,471 Mn, of which growth capex ₹1,696 Mn is discretionary, so **CFO − maintenance capex (₹1,189 Mn) ≈ ₹2,282 Mn** is available for debt service before growth spend | earnings/01_historical-financials (CFO 3,471; capex 2,885; FCF 586); FY26 deck p.16 (maint. capex 1,189 / growth 1,696) |
| Revolver / committed undrawn facility availability | **Unknown** — no committed-undrawn-facility or borrowing-base disclosure in the pool; the bank overdraft/cash-credit line availability is not disclosed, so it is **excluded** from this plan and from liquidity (per module rule) | 00_solvency-data-triage, §3 (committed/undrawn = N); MODULE_RULES True-Liquidity rule |
| Asset-sale proceeds (announced / authorised) | **~₹282 Mn upfront** (75% of ₹376 Mn equity consideration) from the **Milann fertility divestment**, signed, close expected Q1 FY27; balance ₹94 Mn over 18 months; EV ₹632 Mn. **Related-party flag:** buyer (Inviga Healthcare Fund I) is controlled by the promoter/Non-Exec Chairman Dr. B.S. Ajaikumar | Q4/FY26 transcript, prepared remarks; FY26 deck, "Milann Divestment Update" p.~5 |
| New debt issuance (committed / announced) | **None disclosed** — no new committed facility, bond, or ECB announced; HCG has no listed debt and "no ECB by the Company" | FY24-25 AR, p.~93; module 01 §1 |

Rows marked "unknown" / "none" are explicitly so — not assumed to exist.

**Assessment (next 12–24 months).** The dated term-debt maturity inside 12 months is **~₹1.3 Bn** (the rest of the ₹3.1 Bn current line is the rollable overdraft), and cash on hand is **₹5,360.5 Mn** with maintenance-capex-adjusted operating cash of **~₹2.3 Bn/yr**. On the firm data, **cash alone covers the dated near-term term maturities ~4x over**, and cash + maintenance-adjusted CFO covers them comfortably without new market access — so the dated wall is **self-funded**. The qualifier is the **on-demand overdraft (~₹1.78 Bn)**: it is contractually callable, so it depends on continued working-capital line availability (which is undisclosed); in practice it rolls, but it is the one item exposed to a bank pulling a demand line. Rating posture: **ICRA A+ (Stable) long-term / A1+ short-term**, no revision in FY25 — investment-grade-equivalent domestic access [FY24-25 AR, Corporate Governance Report "O. Credit Ratings", p.~155; module 01 §7]; no full rating report is in the pool. Recent refi activity: a **₹4,250 Mn rights issue** (Q4 FY26, oversubscribed 1.3x) and **repayment of some long-term debt**, which cut borrowings and the overdraft [FY26 deck p.14-16; transcript 20 May 2026]. Floating-rate share (~96%) means **almost the entire interest bill reprices** if rates move — but with the repo at 5.25% the near-term repricing is downward.

**Conclusion: "self-funded / low refi risk"** for the dated term maturities (cash + maintenance-adjusted CFO cover them several times over, ratings are A+/A1+, and the refi cost steps *down* at current benchmarks). The single residual exposure is roll of the ~₹1.78 Bn on-demand overdraft, whose committed availability is undisclosed.

---

#### 5. Refinancing Read

The wall is **not a bullet** — it is a flat ~₹1.0–1.3 Bn/yr secured term-loan ladder over 4–10 years plus a long-dated, self-amortising Ind AS 116 lease tail (₹9.5 Bn undiscounted beyond year 4), and the headline "34% due within 12 months" shrinks to a **~₹1.3 Bn dated term maturity** once the ~₹1.78 Bn rollable on-demand overdraft is stripped out; that ₹1.3 Bn is covered ~4x by the ₹5.36 Bn cash pile, so timing risk is low. On cost, because ~96% of the debt is floating and the RBI repo rate has fallen to 5.25% (from ~6.5% when most loans were struck), refinancing/repricing today runs at roughly **7.1–8.1%** versus the current ~8% all-in — an **indicative ≈ −50 bps step-DOWN**, consistent with management's guidance for "some reduction in interest cost." The single biggest refinancing risk is therefore **not the term wall or the cost** but **(a) a rate reversal hitting the ~96% unhedged floating book (a +200 bps shock ≈ −₹170–180 Mn pre-tax, ~3.6–3.9% of EBITDA) and (b) a bank withdrawing the ~₹1.78 Bn on-demand overdraft whose committed availability is undisclosed.**

**Survival under "market closure" (no new unsecured issuance) over the next 12 months:** **Yes, on the best available evidence.** HCG carries ₹5,360.5 Mn cash against ~₹1.3 Bn of dated term maturities and generates ~₹2.3 Bn/yr of maintenance-capex-adjusted operating cash; it has no bonds, no ECB, and no near-term unsecured bullet to refinance, so it does not depend on new unsecured issuance to clear the 12-month wall. The one assumption required: the **on-demand bank overdraft (~₹1.78 Bn) is not called** during the closure — committed availability is undisclosed, so this is labelled an assumption, not a proven fact [00_solvency-data-triage, §3]. The deferred Vizag consideration (₹1,543 Mn) was already paid in Apr-2026 and is outside this window; residual Vizag put/forward liabilities (~₹0.6–1.3 Bn) and the small Milann inflow (~₹282 Mn) are handed to agents 03/05.

**Inverted-score flag:** refinancing-risk severity is an inverted measure (higher = worse). The qualitative read here is **low** for the term wall and cost, **moderate** for the unhedged floating-rate repricing and the undisclosed overdraft-availability exposure. **Confidence is capped at Low** per the partial-data rule (no year-by-year maturity schedule at the FY26 canonical date; the granular table is FY25, one year stale) [MODULE_RULES partial-data table; 00_solvency-data-triage §5].

---

## BSS-03 · liquidity-runway — Liquidity Runway — HCG

### Liquidity Runway — HCG

HealthCare Global Enterprises Limited (NSE: HCG / BSE: 539787). Report date: 2026-06-01. Cancer-care and multispecialty hospital network operator (India + one centre in Kenya). **Reporting currency: INR million (₹ Mn), consolidated, Ind AS**, as at 31 March 2026 (FY26) unless a date is stated. Fiscal year ends 31 March.

**Cross-module inputs read:** `balance-sheet-survival/01_capital-structure-and-leverage.md` (cash, debt, near-term cash claims), `balance-sheet-survival/00_solvency-data-triage.md` (data gaps and caps), `earnings/01_historical-financials.md` (CFO, capex, FCF), `earnings/06_earnings-quality.md` (is CFO cash-backed?).

**Upstream output missing:** `02_maturity-wall-and-refinancing.md` is not yet in the folder — this agent runs in Layer 2 in parallel with `02`. Per the module rule, I proceed with available data and build the 12-month maturity figure directly from the FY26 current/non-current borrowings split in `01` (the same source `02` will use), flagged as a current-portion figure rather than a clean year-by-year wall. When `02` lands, its 12-month maturity figure should replace the figure used here if it differs.

**Partial-data rule applied (two binds):**
1. **No committed undrawn-facility disclosure.** HCG references a working-capital / cash-credit facility (the parent gives a corporate guarantee for "bank guarantee and cash credit facility"), but commitment size, drawings, availability and borrowing base are **not disclosed in the pool** [00_solvency-data-triage, §3; FY24-25 AR, Note 46(c)]. Per `MODULE_RULES.md`, the revolver is excluded from usable liquidity, headline liquidity = **cash + liquid investments only**, and **liquidity is understated** to the extent any undrawn committed capacity exists. **Liquidity runway score capped at 60.**
2. **Cash flow statement is present** (FY23–FY25 audited; FY26 from board-approved audited results deck), so FCF is **not** proxied — the "no cash flow statement" cap does **not** bind. CFO/FCF are used directly.

---

#### 1. Liquidity Sources (committed only)

FY26 (31 March 2026), consolidated, ₹ Mn.

| Source | Amount (₹ Mn) | Usable? | Notes | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Cash & cash equivalents (balance-sheet line) | 5,360.5 | Y | Includes ~₹21 Mn liquid mutual funds (treated as cash-like) | FY26 results deck, Consolidated Balance Sheet p.~28; net-debt note p.~14 |
| Liquid short-term investments | (included above) | Y | The ~₹21 Mn mutual-fund holding is the only liquid investment; already inside the cash line | FY26 results deck, net-debt note p.~14 |
| Bank balance held as margin money | ~287 | **N** | **Restricted** — pledged as margin money against bank guarantees / facilities; the company nets it *out of borrowings*, not into cash. Not freely available. | FY26 results deck, net-debt note footnote 1, p.~14; 01 §3 |
| Revolver / cash-credit facility (commitment) | Not disclosed | **N — availability unknown** | A working-capital / cash-credit line exists (parent corporate guarantee given), but commitment, drawings and availability are not in the pool. Excluded per the True-Liquidity-Availability rule. | 00 §3; FY24-25 AR, Note 46(c) |
| Revolver availability (if disclosed) | Not disclosed | N | Borrowing base / drawings not disclosed | 00 §3 |
| **Total usable liquidity (committed)** | **5,360.5** | | Cash only. Excludes ~₹287 Mn restricted margin money and the undrawn (availability-unknown) cash-credit line. | Derived |

**Notes on this table:**
- **Reporting currency: ₹ Mn, consolidated, Ind AS.** Cash is substantially INR; the Kenya operation is small and not separately quantified for cash-trapping in the pool [01 §6A].
- **Headline usable liquidity = cash only (₹5,360.5 Mn)** because no committed-facility availability is disclosed. To the extent HCG holds undrawn committed cash-credit capacity, **this figure understates true liquidity** — but the rule (and the conservative default) require excluding it until availability is shown.
- The short-term working-capital / bank-overdraft borrowings (₹2,829.3 Mn at FY25, "repayable on demand") are *drawn* and sit inside the debt stack [01 §1] — they are not undrawn capacity and add nothing to liquidity.
- **Restricted-cash flag:** ~₹287 Mn margin money is not usable liquidity and is excluded. No other restricted/offshore/trapped cash is quantified in the pool.
- **Note on the company-defined ₹5,430 Mn cash figure:** management's net-debt slide uses cash "including mutual funds" of ~₹5,430 Mn [FY26 deck, p.~14]. The ₹5,360.5 Mn balance-sheet "cash & cash equivalents" line is the conservative figure used here; the gap is other bank balances the company chooses to count. Using the strict line avoids overstating usable cash.

---

#### 2. Near-Term Uses (next 12 months)

FY27 (year to 31 March 2027) cash obligations, ₹ Mn. Debt and lease maturities are the **principal** current portions from the FY26 balance-sheet split. Cash interest and maintenance capex are FY26 actuals used as the forward run-rate (HCG is growing, so these are if anything light).

| Use | Amount (₹ Mn) | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Debt maturities — current portion of borrowings (principal) | 3,112.2 | FY26 results deck, Balance Sheet p.~28 (current borrowings); 01 §1, §7. **Includes the ~₹2.8 Bn on-demand working-capital / overdraft line, which is typically rolled — see note below.** |
| Lease liabilities — current portion (principal) | 649.7 | FY26 results deck, Balance Sheet p.~28; 01 §1, §2 |
| Cash interest — borrowings (~8% avg cost) | ~762 | FY24-25 AR, Consolidated Cash Flow ("Interest and other borrowing cost paid" ₹761.62 Mn FY25); avg cost ~8% [Q4/FY26 transcript, 20 May 2026]; 01 §5 |
| Cash interest — lease liabilities (Ind AS 116) | ~820 | FY24-25 AR, Consolidated Cash Flow ("Interest paid on lease liability" ₹819.95 Mn FY25); 01 §5 |
| Maintenance capex (recurring) | 1,189 | FY26 results deck, "Net Debt (Pre Ind-AS)" slide (maintenance ₹1,189 Mn vs growth ₹1,696 Mn); earnings/06 §1 |
| Committed dividends | 0 | No dividend ever paid; negative consolidated retained earnings (₹(4,092) Mn FY25) legally preclude dividends [01 §6A; FY24-25 AR, Note 16] |
| Committed buybacks | 0 | None disclosed |
| **Total near-term uses (with growth capex excluded)** | **6,532.9** | Derived (3,112.2 + 649.7 + 762 + 820 + 1,189) |
| Memo: Vizag 2nd-tranche deferred consideration (**paid Apr-2026**) | 1,543 | FY26 results deck, net-debt bridge p.~14, p.~16; 01 §2. A real near-term cash call already settled in the FY27 window. |
| Memo: residual Vizag put/forward acquisition liabilities | ~600–1,347 | FY24-25 AR, Note 45.1; 01 §2. Future (mostly >12-month) cash claims; the put (₹634 Mn at FY25) sits in the >4-year bucket [FY24-25 AR, Note 40, consolidated]. |
| **Total near-term uses including the Apr-2026 Vizag payment** | **8,075.9** | Derived (6,532.9 + 1,543) |

**Critical notes on near-term uses:**
- **The ₹3,112.2 Mn "current borrowings" is dominated by the on-demand working-capital/overdraft line (~₹2.8 Bn).** Classified current because it is repayable on demand, but in practice it is a continuously-rolled secured working-capital facility, not a hard amortising maturity. **Treating the full ₹3,112.2 Mn as a 12-month repayment is the conservative reading** and overstates the genuine refinancing call; the true hard-amortisation portion (term-loan current maturities) was only ~₹917 Mn of principal at FY25 [01 §1]. Both readings are shown in §3.
- **Cross-check against the FY25 consolidated contractual-maturity table** (the closest thing to a wall in the pool): <1-year **borrowings** contractual cash outflow = ₹4,020.81 Mn and **lease** = ₹1,341.86 Mn — but these are *contractual outflows including future interest*, not principal, so they are not additive to the separate interest lines above [FY24-25 AR, Note 40 (consolidated), 31 Mar 2025]. The FY26 balance-sheet principal split (₹3,112.2 / ₹649.7) is the cleaner basis for the uses table; the contractual table is shown only to corroborate the order of magnitude.
- **Vizag (₹1,543 Mn) is a one-off acquisition cash call already paid in April 2026.** It is not a recurring obligation, so it is held as a memo and shown as a sensitivity rather than baked into the base near-term-uses run-rate; but because it falls inside the FY27 window it is a real first-quarter draw on the ₹5,360.5 Mn cash balance.

---

#### 3. Runway

EBITDA basis: Reported EBITDA ₹4,657.9 Mn (FY26). FCF = CFO − total capex per CLAUDE.md; HCG publishes no own FCF figure. CFO is cash-backed (CFO/Reported EBITDA = 74.5% FY26, above the 70% healthy line for four straight years) [earnings/06 §1-2].

| Metric | Value (₹ Mn) |
|---|---:|
| Total committed liquidity (cash only) | 5,360.5 |
| Annual CFO (FY26) | 3,471 |
| Annual FCF — headline (CFO − total capex 2,885) | 586 |
| Annual FCF — on maintenance capex only (CFO − maint capex 1,189) | 2,282 |
| Total near-term uses, **ex-Vizag, ex-growth capex** (from §2) | 6,532.9 |
| Net near-term obligations = uses − headline FCF | 5,946.9 |
| Net near-term obligations = uses − maintenance-capex FCF | 4,250.9 |

**Runway formula:** Liquidity runway (months) = Total committed liquidity ÷ (net near-term cash burn ÷ 12), where net near-term cash burn = near-term uses − annual FCF.

**Two runway reads (the FCF definition drives the answer):**

1. **On headline FCF (₹586 Mn — i.e. assuming HCG keeps spending ₹1,696 Mn/yr of discretionary growth capex):**
   - Net burn = 6,532.9 − 586 = ₹5,946.9 Mn/yr.
   - Runway = 5,360.5 ÷ (5,946.9 ÷ 12) = 5,360.5 ÷ 495.6 = **~10.8 months.**

2. **On maintenance-capex FCF (₹2,282 Mn — i.e. growth capex paused, the right basis for a survival/runway test):**
   - Net burn = 6,532.9 − 2,282 = ₹4,250.9 Mn/yr.
   - Runway = 5,360.5 ÷ (4,250.9 ÷ 12) = 5,360.5 ÷ 354.2 = **~15.1 months.**

**Stripping the rolled working-capital line (the more economically accurate read).** If the ~₹2.8 Bn on-demand overdraft is rolled (its normal behaviour) rather than repaid, the genuine hard 12-month uses fall to roughly: term-loan principal ~₹917 Mn + lease principal ₹649.7 Mn + cash interest ~₹1,582 Mn + maintenance capex ₹1,189 Mn = **~₹4,338 Mn**. Against maintenance-capex FCF of ₹2,282 Mn, net burn ≈ ₹2,056 Mn/yr → **cash alone covers ~31 months**, and FCF on a maintenance basis (₹2,282 Mn) covers ~53% of these uses outright, so the position is **internally fundable with a wide margin** on this basis.

**Headline runway selected: ~15 months on a maintenance-capex basis (committed cash ₹5,360.5 Mn against net burn of ₹4,250.9 Mn/yr), and effectively open-ended (~31 months on cash, FCF-covered) once the rolled working-capital line is treated as rolled rather than repaid.** The ~10.8-month figure is the conservative outer bound that assumes both (a) the full overdraft is repaid and (b) HCG keeps spending all of its discretionary growth capex — two things that do not both happen in a genuine liquidity squeeze.

**Vizag overlay (Apr-2026, ₹1,543 Mn):** this one-off acquisition payment, made in the first month of FY27, reduces opening cash from ₹5,360.5 Mn to ~₹3,818 Mn. On the maintenance-capex net-burn of ₹4,250.9 Mn/yr that shortens the conservative runway to ~10.8 months; on the rolled-overdraft basis (~₹2,056 Mn net burn) it still leaves ~22 months of cash and remains FCF-covered. The Vizag payment is a known, funded, one-off — not a recurring drain — but it is the single biggest discrete first-half-FY27 call on cash.

##### Seasonality / Peak Liquidity Need (Hard Check)

Working capital is **not materially seasonal**: each fiscal quarter is ~24–26% of annual revenue, with only a mild back-half tilt (Q4 ~26%, Q1 ~24%) and no quarter breaching the 30%/20% thresholds [earnings/01 §5]. There is therefore no large intra-year seasonal working-capital build that would create a peak-quarter cash trough materially above the annual-average run-rate.

The relevant non-seasonal swing is the **structural working-capital drain**: working capital has absorbed cash every year (−₹272 Mn to −₹765 Mn; FY26 −₹426 Mn), driven by receivable build (government-scheme / insurance / corporate oncology billing; DSO 56→66→60 days FY24–FY26) [earnings/06 §1, §3]. This is already inside CFO and the FCF figures above, so it is captured, not additive. **A specific peak-quarter working-capital cash requirement is not separately disclosed in the pool — to that extent the runway may be modestly overstated if a single quarter (e.g. a state-scheme collection delay like the Q3FY26 Andhra Pradesh disruption) draws cash ahead of the annual average.** Given the absence of seasonality, the overstatement risk is low.

---

#### 4. Sources & Uses Bridge

On a maintenance-capex basis, **internal sources cover the next 12 months without needing the capital markets**: opening cash of ₹5,360.5 Mn plus maintenance-FCF of ~₹2,282 Mn (≈₹7.6 Bn of internal liquidity) exceeds genuine hard 12-month uses of ~₹4.3 Bn (term-loan principal + lease principal + cash interest + maintenance capex), even after the ₹1,543 Mn Vizag payment — *provided the ~₹2.8 Bn on-demand working-capital line is rolled, which is its normal behaviour and which depends on the secured lenders continuing to extend it.* External access (refinancing, asset sale, or a drawdown) is therefore **not required to survive**, but it **is required to keep growing**: HCG's ₹1,696 Mn/yr discretionary growth capex and any further M&A are funded out of the same cash that the rights issue just topped up, not out of free cash flow (headline FCF is only ₹586 Mn).

On the split between in-hand vs must-materialise: **the runway is predominantly in-hand.** Of the ~₹7.6 Bn of internal liquidity, ₹5,360.5 Mn (~70%) is cash already on the balance sheet from the Q4 FY26 ₹4,250 Mn rights issue, and only ~₹2.3 Bn (~30%) is maintenance-basis FCF that must still be generated over FY27. The cash cushion is real and recently funded; the FCF that must materialise is well-supported (74.5% cash conversion, four-year track record), so the must-materialise portion is lower-risk than the in-hand portion is large. The genuine dependency is **not** FCF holding up — it is **the secured working-capital line continuing to roll** (availability undisclosed) and **lenders refinancing the ~₹917 Mn of term-loan amortisation**, neither of which is contractually committed in the pool.

---

#### 5. Liquidity Read

On a maintenance-capex basis the runway is **~15 months of committed cash against net cash burn, and effectively open-ended (FCF-covered, ~31 months on cash) once the ~₹2.8 Bn on-demand working-capital line is treated as rolled rather than repaid** — HCG can meet hard near-term obligations from the ₹5,360.5 Mn cash pile (raised in the Q4 FY26 ₹4,250 Mn rights issue) plus ~₹2.3 Bn of maintenance FCF without tapping the capital markets, even after the ₹1,543 Mn Vizag payment made in April 2026. The runway is mostly **already-in-hand** (~70% cash, ~30% must-materialise FCF that is well-supported by 74.5% cash conversion), so survival does not hinge on FCF; it hinges on the rolled working-capital facility continuing to roll and on ~₹917 Mn of annual term-loan amortisation being refinanced. **The single biggest liquidity risk is the undisclosed committed-facility availability:** because no undrawn-revolver capacity is disclosed, usable liquidity is pinned to cash only, and the largest "current" obligation (the ~₹2.8 Bn on-demand overdraft) could in principle be pulled exactly when needed — so the headline runway is conservative on uses but blind on the backstop.

---

##### Self-Check
- Liquidity uses **committed facilities only**; the cash-credit/revolver is excluded (availability unknown) and noted; uncommitted/undrawn lines are not counted. ✓
- Restricted cash (~₹287 Mn margin money) flagged and excluded. ✓
- Near-term uses pull the 12-month maturity figure from the FY26 current/non-current split (the source `02` will use); `02` not yet available — noted, and the contractual-maturity table used as a cross-check. ✓
- Runway expressed in months with the formula shown; two FCF bases and a rolled-overdraft read given; the FCF-surplus case (rolled overdraft) stated. ✓
- Split between in-hand liquidity (~70%) and must-materialise FCF (~30%) stated. ✓
- Partial-data rule applied: **no undrawn-facility disclosure → liquidity = cash only, understated, Liquidity runway score capped at 60.** Cash-flow statement present → FCF not proxied, that cap not triggered. ✓
- No banned phrases (no naked "adequate liquidity" / "comfortable" / "strong balance sheet"). ✓

---

## BSS-04 · coverage-and-covenants — Coverage & Covenants — HCG

### Coverage & Covenants — HCG

HealthCare Global Enterprises Limited (NSE: HCG / BSE: 539787). Report date: 2026-06-01. Cancer-care / multispecialty hospital operator (India + one centre in Kenya). **Reporting currency: INR million (₹ Mn), consolidated, Ind AS.** Fiscal year ends 31 March (FY26 = year ended 31 Mar 2026). Operating company with corporate term-loan debt plus Ind AS 116 lease liabilities — the coverage/covenant framework applies [01_capital-structure-and-leverage; FY24-25 AR, Note 18].

**Inputs used.** Debt and the EBITDA base are taken verbatim from `01_capital-structure-and-leverage.md`. EBIT, EBITDA, finance cost and capex are from the **board-approved audited FY26 results presentation dated 19 May 2026** [FY26 deck, P&L p.19; Capex/Net Debt p.14]. The finance-cost split (lease vs non-lease interest) is from the **FY25 statutory borrowings/finance-cost note** [FY24-25 AR, Note 28], because the FY26 deck reports only the single finance-cost line and does not break it out — the FY26 split is therefore proxied and flagged. Cash-conversion quality is from `earnings/06_earnings-quality.md`.

**EBITDA basis and interest convention (stated up front).** EBITDA throughout is **Reported EBITDA ₹4,657.9 Mn** (audited statutory operating result, pre other-income); **Adjusted EBITDA ₹4,711.3 Mn** (company-defined: Reported + ESOP ₹14.4 Mn + one-time ₹39.0 Mn) is shown alongside but differs by only +₹53 Mn (+1.15%), so Reported is the conservative default [FY26 deck, p.19; earnings/06 §7]. EBITDA is **post-Ind AS 116** (leases capitalised). Interest is **gross finance cost**; the company does not net interest income against finance cost in the P&L. Because Ind AS 116 routes a large lease-interest charge through finance cost, coverage is shown on **two interest bases**: (i) total finance cost incl. lease interest (the like-for-like, post-Ind AS view), and (ii) cash borrowing interest only, ex-lease (proxied) — the two diverge materially and both are reported to avoid a misleading single number.

---

#### 1. Coverage Ratios

FY26 (year ended 31 Mar 2026), consolidated, ₹ Mn. Inputs: Reported EBITDA 4,657.9; EBIT 2,466.1; total finance cost 1,765.7; total capex 2,885.0; maintenance capex 1,189.0 [FY26 deck, p.19; p.14].

| Ratio | Value | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| **EBITDA / interest** (interest = total finance cost incl. lease) | **2.64x** | 4,657.9 ÷ 1,765.7 [FY26 deck, p.19] |
| EBITDA / interest (Adjusted EBITDA basis) | 2.67x | 4,711.3 ÷ 1,765.7 [FY26 deck, p.19] |
| **EBIT / interest** (incl. lease interest) | **1.40x** | 2,466.1 ÷ 1,765.7 [FY26 deck, p.19] |
| **(EBITDA − total capex) / interest** (incl. lease interest) | **1.00x** | (4,657.9 − 2,885.0) ÷ 1,765.7 [FY26 deck, p.19, p.14] |
| (EBITDA − maintenance capex) / interest (incl. lease) | 1.96x | (4,657.9 − 1,189.0) ÷ 1,765.7 [FY26 deck, p.14, p.19] |
| **Fixed-charge coverage** = (EBITDA − total capex) ÷ (finance cost + scheduled amort + lease principal) | **0.50x** | (4,657.9 − 2,885.0) ÷ (1,765.7 + 1,147.1 + 649.7); amort proxy from FY25 current LT-debt portion, lease principal = FY26 current lease liability 649.7 [FY26 deck p.14; FY24-25 AR Note 18; FY26 deck BS p.20] |
| Fixed-charge coverage (maintenance-capex version) | 0.97x | (4,657.9 − 1,189.0) ÷ 3,562.5 |
| Memo: fixed-charge coverage excl. capex = EBITDA ÷ (fin cost + amort + lease principal) | 1.31x | 4,657.9 ÷ 3,562.5 |
| **Memo — coverage on cash (ex-lease) interest only** (proxy) | | |
| EBITDA / cash borrowing interest (ex-lease ~₹970 Mn proxy) | ~4.8x | 4,657.9 ÷ ~970; see proxy note below |
| EBIT / cash borrowing interest (ex-lease proxy) | ~2.5x | 2,466.1 ÷ ~970 |
| (EBITDA − maintenance capex) / cash interest (ex-lease proxy) | ~3.6x | (4,657.9 − 1,189.0) ÷ ~970 |
| **Company-disclosed DSCR** (Schedule III, standalone) | **1.66x (FY25); 2.06x (FY24)** | FY24-25 AR, Note 50 (standalone Ratios) — see definition below |

**Interest-basis note (critical to reading these ratios).** The FY26 single finance-cost line of ₹1,765.7 Mn includes a large Ind AS 116 lease-interest component. The FY25 statutory note splits finance cost into **non-lease (cash borrowing) interest ₹848.75 Mn** and **lease interest (net, in finance cost) ₹696.86 Mn** — i.e. **~45% of FY25 finance cost was lease interest, not cash interest on borrowings** [FY24-25 AR, Note 28]. The FY26 deck does not publish this split. *Proxy, not from filings: carrying the FY25 45.1% lease share forward implies FY26 cash borrowing interest of ~₹970 Mn and lease interest ~₹796 Mn; scaling FY25 lease interest by the broadly flat lease balance gives a similar ~₹760 Mn. The ex-lease ("cash interest") coverage figures above use ~₹970 Mn and are therefore approximate.* The practical read: on a **cash-debt-service** basis (interest actually paid on borrowings), coverage is roughly **4.8x EBITDA / cash interest** — materially above the 2.64x lease-inclusive figure — because nearly half the "interest" is the unwinding of capitalised rent, not bank interest. This is corroborated by management: quarterly interest cost "~INR40 crore" (₹400 Mn × 4 ≈ ₹1,600 Mn) is the *total* finance-cost run-rate, and the average interest **cost on borrowings is ~8%** on ₹9,104 Mn ≈ ₹730 Mn of pure bank interest [Q4/FY26 transcript, Q&A, 20 May 2026]. Both bases are legitimate; the lease-inclusive 2.64x is the conservative anchor and the cash-interest ~4.8x is the practical debt-service reality.

**EBITDA cash-backing caveat (from earnings/06).** Coverage above uses Reported EBITDA. Per `earnings/06_earnings-quality.md`, EBITDA is **cash-backed but the conversion rate is eroding**: CFO/Reported EBITDA was 74.5% in FY26 (down from 84–86% in FY23–FY24), as working capital drained cash every year and a FY25 receivable build (DSO 56→66 days, receivables +36% vs revenue +16%) only partly reversed [earnings/06 §1–2, §9]. EBITDA is therefore not materially above cash-backed EBITDA (the adjusted-vs-reported EBITDA gap is only +1.15%), but the EBITDA-to-cash slippage means the **CFO that actually services debt is ~25% below EBITDA** — so the (EBITDA−capex)/interest of 1.00x is a fair, not a flattering, gauge of cash debt-service capacity, and the heavy FY26 growth capex (₹1,696 Mn of the ₹2,885 Mn) is the swing factor (maintenance-capex coverage is 1.96x) [earnings/06 §1].

---

#### 2. Covenant Inventory

**No financial maintenance covenant is disclosed in the data pool.** The FY25 Annual Report — which contains the full statutory borrowings note (Note 18, with security and repayment terms) and the auditor's report — discloses **no maximum-leverage, minimum-interest-coverage, minimum-net-worth or minimum-liquidity maintenance covenant** for any facility [FY24-25 AR, Note 18; searched in full]. The only "covenant" wording in the document is boilerplate in the Stakeholders' Relationship Committee charter referring to debenture-holder security cover [FY24-25 AR, Corporate Governance Report, p.~134] — and **HCG has no debentures/listed debt outstanding** ("no External Commercial Borrowing by the Company"; no bonds/notes) [FY24-25 AR, p.~93; 01_capital-structure §1]. Term loans are bilateral bank facilities; their specific financial covenants (if any) are not reproduced in the filing.

Per the module partial-data rule, indicative thresholds below are **LABELED ASSUMPTIONS** for an A+ (ICRA)-rated Indian operating company, used only to compute indicative headroom. **True covenant headroom cannot be computed and is marked "Not assessable" for scoring.**

| Covenant | Threshold | Current Actual | Headroom | Source |
|---|---|---:|---:|---|
| Max net leverage (lender) | **Not disclosed** | Net debt incl. lease / EBITDA 2.57x; ex-lease 0.80x | **Not assessable** | FY24-25 AR, Note 18 (no covenant stated) |
| Min interest coverage (lender) | **Not disclosed** | EBITDA / interest 2.64x | **Not assessable** | FY24-25 AR, Note 18 (no covenant stated) |
| Min liquidity / net worth (lender) | **Not disclosed** | Net worth (equity) ₹14,102.1 Mn; cash ₹5,360.5 Mn | **Not assessable** | FY26 deck, BS p.20 |
| Springing covenant (e.g. revolver-utilisation trigger) | **Not disclosed** | Working-capital/overdraft line is "repayable on demand"; no springing test disclosed | **Not assessable** | FY24-25 AR, Note 18.1.4 |
| Equity cure rights | **Not disclosed** | n/a | **Not assessable** | FY24-25 AR, Note 18 |
| Change-of-control / cross-default / rating triggers | **Not disclosed in the data pool** | KKR took sole control 30 May 2025 via open offer; no debt-side CoC put or rating-step disclosed | n/a | FY24-25 AR, p.~93 (change in control & open offer); 01_capital-structure §6A |
| **Memo — company-disclosed DSCR (Schedule III, not a lender covenant)** | Disclosure only (no minimum) | **1.66x (FY25)**, 2.06x (FY24) | n/a — no threshold | FY24-25 AR, Note 50 (standalone) |
| **Memo — management's self-imposed leverage ceiling** | **2.5x** net debt/EBITDA, **ex-lease** (internal target, not a lender covenant) | 0.80x (BS net ex-lease) / 0.73x (company-headline) / 1.43x (Vizag-adjusted) | +68% to +71% vs the 2.5x internal ceiling | Q4/FY26 transcript, Q&A, 20 May 2026 |

**The only quantified anchors are self-imposed or disclosure-only, not lender tests.** Management states an **internal** net-debt/EBITDA ceiling of **2.5x** (ex-lease basis) and that leverage came down "from 2.2x, 2.3x to 1.4x" after the ₹4,250 Mn rights issue [Q4/FY26 transcript, Q&A]. This is a board-level discipline, not a covenant a lender can act on. The Schedule III DSCR (FY25 1.66x) is a regulatory ratio disclosure under the Companies Act, again not a maintenance test. There is therefore **no external trigger that a lender can pull on a leverage or coverage deterioration** that is visible in the pool — which cuts both ways: there is no near-term breach risk from a disclosed covenant, but there is also no disclosed covenant protection or early-warning mechanism for creditors, and the bilateral bank loans almost certainly carry undisclosed covenants that this analysis cannot see.

##### Covenant EBITDA Definition & Quality

Because **no lender covenant headroom is computed**, a covenant-EBITDA definition cannot be assessed.

| Item | Value / Description | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Covenant EBITDA definition summary | **Unknown — no lender covenant or its EBITDA definition is disclosed.** | FY24-25 AR, Note 18 |
| Addbacks permitted (types) | Unknown | — |
| Addback caps / limits | Unknown | — |
| Is covenant EBITDA materially above reported EBITDA? | Cannot be determined. For the *company's own* leverage/DSCR metrics, the EBITDA used is Reported/Adjusted EBITDA (adjusted is only +1.15% above reported), so "addback illusion" risk in the company-disclosed ratios is **low**; for any undisclosed lender covenant it is **unknown**. | FY26 deck, p.19; earnings/06 §7 |

**Headroom quality is unknown** to the extent any undisclosed lender covenant exists. For the *visible* metrics (internal 2.5x ceiling, Schedule III DSCR), the EBITDA base is close to reported EBITDA, so those specific figures are not inflated by aggressive addbacks.

---

#### 3. Headroom & Breach Proximity

Because no lender covenant is disclosed, "tightest covenant" below is assessed against (a) the **only quantified internal constraint** management states (2.5x ex-lease net leverage), and (b) **labeled-assumption** market covenants, shown separately and not used for scoring.

| Metric | Value |
|---|---:|
| Tightest *disclosed/quantified* constraint | **Management's internal 2.5x ex-lease net-leverage ceiling** (self-imposed; not a lender covenant) |
| Headroom on that constraint | **+68%** (actual 0.80x ex-lease net vs 2.5x); +71% on the company-headline 0.73x basis |
| EBITDA decline that would breach the 2.5x ex-lease ceiling (net debt held constant) | ~−68% (EBITDA would have to fall from ₹4,658 Mn to ~₹1,497 Mn) — but ex-lease net debt is only ₹3,744 Mn, so in practice cash burn would move net debt too |
| Debt increase that would breach the 2.5x ex-lease ceiling (EBITDA held constant) | +₹7,901 Mn of net debt (net debt would have to roughly triple, from ₹3,744 Mn to ₹11,645 Mn) |
| **Tightest *assumed* lender covenant** (labeled assumption — not assessable for scoring) | **Assumed min interest coverage 2.0x** (lease-inclusive interest) |
| Indicative headroom vs assumed min coverage 2.0x | +32% (actual EBITDA/interest 2.64x) |
| EBITDA decline that would breach an assumed 2.0x coverage (interest held constant) | ~−24% (EBITDA to ~₹3,531 Mn) |
| Indicative headroom vs assumed max net leverage 3.5x (incl. lease) | +26% (actual 2.57x) |
| EBITDA decline that would breach an assumed 3.5x net leverage (net debt held constant) | ~−26% (EBITDA to ~₹3,427 Mn) |

**Read of breach proximity.** Against the internal 2.5x ceiling, headroom is wide (+68%) and only a near-tripling of net debt or a ~two-thirds EBITDA collapse would breach it — i.e. it is not a near-term constraint. **If** the bilateral loans carry typical covenants (assumed min-coverage 2.0x / max-net-leverage 3.5x incl. lease), the indicative tightest test is interest coverage, which would trip on roughly a **−24% EBITDA decline** holding interest constant — a plausible downside given oncology-volume sensitivity and state-scheme collection risk [business-model/10; earnings/06 §3]. The honest conclusion: the company looks far from any *disclosed* constraint, but the absence of covenant disclosure means a real lender test could sit closer than the internal ceiling implies, and the stress agent (06) should run breach checks against both the assumed 2.0x coverage and 3.5x leverage lines.

---

#### 4. Coverage / Covenant Read

Earnings carry interest with a moderate, not wide, cushion on the conservative (lease-inclusive) basis: **EBITDA/interest is 2.64x and EBIT/interest is 1.40x** [FY26 deck, p.19] — but ~45% of the ₹1,765.7 Mn finance cost is Ind AS 116 lease interest, so on **cash bank-interest only the coverage is roughly 4.8x**, and after total growth-and-maintenance capex it drops to **1.00x** (1.96x on maintenance capex alone) [FY24-25 AR, Note 28; FY26 deck, p.14]. **No lender financial covenant is disclosed in the pool**, so true covenant headroom is **Not assessable** for scoring (partial-data cap applied); the only quantified anchors are management's self-imposed 2.5x ex-lease net-leverage ceiling — against which leverage of 0.80x sits ~68% below — and a Schedule III DSCR of 1.66x (FY25), neither of which a lender can act on. The practical risk is not a visible covenant breach but the combination of an **eroding cash conversion (CFO/EBITDA 74.5%, down from 86%)**, heavy growth capex that has pushed (EBITDA−capex)/interest to break-even, and the fact that an undisclosed bilateral-loan coverage covenant (if it exists at a typical ~2.0x) would trip on a ~−24% EBITDA decline — so the survival floor depends on a covenant the pool cannot show, which agent 06 must stress against labeled assumptions.

---

##### Self-check / data flags

- All four coverage ratios computed (EBITDA/interest 2.64x; EBIT/interest 1.40x; (EBITDA−capex)/interest 1.00x; fixed-charge coverage 0.50x), on Reported EBITDA, gross interest, lease-inclusive basis; cash/ex-lease coverage shown as a flagged proxy. ✓
- EBITDA basis (Reported, post-Ind AS 116) and gross/lease-inclusive interest convention stated. ✓
- EBITDA cash-backing caveated using `earnings/06` (CFO/EBITDA 74.5%, eroding; adjusted-vs-reported gap +1.15%). ✓
- **Partial data:** No lender covenant disclosure → labeled-assumption approach applied; covenant headroom marked **"Not assessable"** for scoring. No FY26 finance-cost split → lease/cash-interest split proxied from FY25 Note 28 and flagged. (Per MODULE_RULES partial-data + score-cap tables: no covenant disclosure → covenant headroom "Not assessable", overall usefulness max 75; no interest-expense detail → coverage proxied and flagged.)
- Tightest *quantified* constraint (internal 2.5x ceiling, +68% headroom) and the move that would trip it (net debt ~3x or EBITDA −68%) identified; assumed-covenant breach proximities (coverage 2.0x at −24% EBITDA) handed to agent 06. ✓
- No banned phrases used (no naked "comfortable coverage" / "ample headroom"; every headroom and coverage figure is stated as a number). ✓

---

## BSS-05 · off-balance-sheet-and-contingencies — Off-Balance-Sheet & Contingencies — HCG

### Off-Balance-Sheet & Contingencies — HCG

HealthCare Global Enterprises Limited (NSE: HCG / BSE: 539787). Report date: 2026-06-01. India-focused cancer-care and multispecialty hospital operator (≈25 hospitals; one cancer centre in Kenya). **Reporting currency: INR million (₹ Mn), consolidated, Ind AS.** Fiscal year ends 31 March (FY25 = year ended 31 Mar 2025; FY26 = year ended 31 Mar 2026).

**Source basis (read this first).** The latest *audited statutory* commitments-and-contingencies disclosure in the pool is the **FY24-25 Annual Report** (year ended 31 Mar 2025): consolidated Note 33 (Contingent liabilities), Note 34 (Commitments), Note 37 (Employee benefit plans), Note 19/45.1 (put / forward liabilities), and standalone Note 46(c) (corporate guarantees). The **FY26 statutory contingencies note is not in the pool** — only the 19 May 2026 board-results deck and the 20 May 2026 earnings call are available for FY26, and neither re-states a full commitments-and-contingencies schedule. Recognized balance-sheet items (put/forward liabilities, gratuity) are therefore shown at their FY25 statutory values; the FY26 cash event (Vizag deferred consideration paid Apr-2026) is carried in from the FY26 deck and `01`. **Partial-data caveat:** the most recent contingent-liability quantum is one year stale, and for a serial acquirer with a tax-litigation tail, undisclosed FY26 movements cannot be ruled out (see Section 5 and the partial-data flag).

**Cross-module inputs read:** `01_capital-structure-and-leverage.md` (to avoid double-counting items already in the debt stack); `business-model/11_capital-allocation-governance.md` (off-balance-sheet / related-party context); `business-model/10_external-dependency.md` (policy/regulatory and EPCG/export-obligation context). Valuation cross-module input not available — valuation module not yet run; proceeding on this module's own read of the data pool.

**Boundary note:** this agent does not value the company, assign probabilities, or rate the stock. It records recognized vs maximum exposures and flags double-counting with `01`. Stress and verdict belong to agents 06 and 99.

---

#### 1. Off-Balance-Sheet / Debt-Like Obligations

| Item | Recognized Liability (₹ Mn) | Maximum / Gross Exposure (₹ Mn) | Already in 01's debt? | Source |
|---|---:|---:|---|---|
| Operating leases | 8,249.5 (FY26 lease liabilities, NC 7,599.8 + Cur 649.7) | Same — capitalised | **YES — already in 01's gross debt incl. leases** | FY26 deck, Balance Sheet; FY24-25 AR, Note 6/6.1 |
| — Genuinely off-balance-sheet lease commitments (not yet capitalised) | n/a | 239.85 (lease commitments, consolidated) | No | FY24-25 AR, Note 34 (Commitments) |
| Pension / gratuity (defined-benefit) underfunding | 257.71 (net DB liability; DBO 273.81 − plan assets 16.10) | 273.81 (gross DBO, if plan assets vanished) | No (sits in provisions, not borrowings) | FY24-25 AR, Note 37 (consolidated) |
| Securitisation / factoring / receivables sold with recourse | None disclosed | None | No | FY24-25 AR, accounting policy (recourse features mention only — no programme); no securitisation note found |
| Purchase / capital commitments (contracts to be executed, not provided for) | nil (commitment, not a liability) | 1,975.24 (capital account, consolidated) | No | FY24-25 AR, Note 34 (Commitments) |
| Written put options to NCI of subsidiaries (Vizag VHCRPL + Medi-Surge) | 634.00 (gross obligation, recognised in Note 19 other financial liabilities, NC) | 1,778.00 (written put options — commitment-note / gross figure) | No (other financial liability, NOT in 01's borrowings) | FY24-25 AR, Note 19 (recognised 634.00); Note 34 (commitment 1,778.00); Note 45.1 |
| Forward liability — obligation to acquire additional 34% of Vizag (VHCRPL) | 1,401.34 (recognised in Note 19 other financial liabilities, Current) | 1,540.00 (committed acquisition price for the 34%; "forward liability" in Note 34 = 1,540.00) | No (other financial liability, NOT in 01's borrowings) | FY24-25 AR, Note 19 (recognised 1,401.34); Note 34 (1,540.00); Note 8.1 / 45.1 |
| Vizag deferred (2nd-tranche) acquisition consideration — **paid Apr-2026** | — (part of the 1,540 / forward above) | 1,543 (cash outflow Apr-2026) | No — post-balance-sheet cash call | FY26 deck, "Capital Expenditure and Net Debt" (₹1,543 Mn paid Apr'26); 01 §2 |
| Vizag residual 15% acquisition (price TBD per SHA) | Not separately quantified | Not quantified — price set per shareholder agreement | No | FY24-25 AR, Note 8.1 (15% "determined as per terms of the SHA") |

State the reporting currency: **INR million (₹ Mn), consolidated, Ind AS.**

**Notes on this section.**
1. **Leases are NOT off-balance-sheet here.** India reports under Ind AS 116 (IFRS-equivalent), so leases are capitalised. The ₹8,249.5 Mn lease liability is already inside `01`'s "gross debt incl. leases" (₹17,353.6 Mn) — it must **not** be added again. Only the ₹239.85 Mn of *additional* lease commitments (Note 34, not yet on the balance sheet) is genuinely off-balance-sheet, and it is small.
2. **The Vizag put + forward are the largest debt-like off-balance-sheet items.** They are recognised financial liabilities (Note 19: put ₹634.00 Mn NC + forward ₹1,401.34 Mn Cur = ₹2,035.34 Mn), but they sit **outside** `01`'s interest-bearing borrowings line — so they are real future cash claims that the leverage ratios in `01` do not capture. The commitment-note (gross/maximum) versions are higher: written put options ₹1,778.00 Mn and forward ₹1,540.00 Mn [FY24-25 AR, Note 34]. Aggregate recognised ≈ ₹2.04 Bn; aggregate gross/maximum (put + forward commitment) ≈ ₹3.32 Bn, before the undetermined residual 15%.
3. **₹1,543 Mn of the forward has already crystallised** — paid in April 2026 (post the 31 Mar 2026 balance sheet). It is no longer contingent; it is a confirmed near-term cash outflow that `01` and the FY26 deck both flag. The remaining put (Medi-Surge/Vizag residual) and the 15% Vizag tranche are still pending.
4. **Gratuity is essentially unfunded but small.** Net DB liability ₹257.71 Mn = ~0.06x FY26 reported EBITDA (4,657.9). Plan assets are negligible (₹16.10 Mn), so the gross DBO (₹273.81 Mn) is the realistic maximum. Not a solvency driver.
5. **No securitisation or off-balance-sheet SPV.** No factoring/securitisation programme is disclosed; the only "recourse" reference is a generic ECL accounting-policy line, not a financing arrangement. Business-model `11` independently concludes "no hidden off-balance-sheet vehicles." Consistent.

---

#### 2. Guarantees & Letters of Credit

| Item | Recorded (₹ Mn) | Maximum Exposure (₹ Mn) | Beneficiary / Purpose | Source |
|---|---:|---:|---|---|
| Corporate guarantees — parent → subsidiary LLPs, for their bank **term loans** | Fair value recognised within investment in subsidiaries (Ind AS 109); not separately quantified as a P&L liability | 631.57 (down from 960.45 at FY24) | HCG Oncology LLP 190.05; HCG Manavata 243.44; HCG Kolkata Cancer Care 164.58; HCG Rajkot 33.50 — term-loan support to lenders | FY24-25 AR, standalone Note 46(c); consolidated Note 33(a) |
| Corporate guarantee — parent → subsidiaries, for **bank-guarantee & cash-credit facilities** | Not disclosed | **Not disclosed / not quantified** | Explicitly excluded from the 631.57 figure ("does not include corporate guarantee given by the Company for bank guarantee and cash credit facility") | FY24-25 AR, Note 46(c) footnote |
| Standby letters of credit | None disclosed | None disclosed | — | No SBLC line in Note 33/34 |
| Financial guarantees to third parties (non-group) | None disclosed | None | — | Guarantees are intra-group only (Note 46c) |
| Performance / surety bonds | None disclosed | None disclosed | — | Not disclosed in the pool |
| Letter of support / "keep-well" to 13 subsidiary entities (going-concern support for ≥1 year from 24 May 2025) | Not quantified (open-ended commitment) | **Unquantified — open-ended** | Operational + financial assistance so listed subsidiaries/LLPs can operate as going concerns | FY24-25 AR, standalone Note 33(viii) |

**Notes on this section.**
1. **Guarantees are intra-group and eliminate on consolidation.** The ₹631.57 Mn corporate guarantee is a **standalone (parent)** contingent liability supporting subsidiary LLP term loans. On a consolidated basis those loans are already inside `01`'s consolidated borrowings, so the guarantee does not add a *new* consolidated cash claim — it is a structural credit-support fact, not incremental leverage. It matters for where the parent's risk concentrates, not for the group total. It is **not** double-counted in `01`.
2. **Two undisclosed gaps.** (a) The guarantee for subsidiary **bank-guarantee and cash-credit facilities** is explicitly *excluded* from the ₹631.57 Mn and **not quantified anywhere in the pool**. (b) The **letter of support to 13 subsidiary entities** (Note 33(viii)) is an open-ended going-concern commitment with no ceiling. Both are real parent-level obligations that cannot be sized from the data — flagged, not invented.
3. **Direction:** disclosed term-loan guarantees fell 34% YoY (960.45 → 631.57) as subsidiary loans were repaid/withdrawn — a declining exposure on the one line that is quantified.
