---

## MG-06 · candor-and-disclosure-quality — Candor & Disclosure Quality — HCG

### Candor & Disclosure Quality — HCG

*HealthCare Global Enterprises Limited (NSE: HCG / BSE: 539787). Indian SEBI-LODR issuer, Ind AS, FY ends 31 March. Reporting currency INR (₹; management often uses ₹ crore — 1 crore = 10 Mn). Report date: 2026-06-01. This agent judges how management communicates when results are bad — owning misses vs obscuring them with adjustments and spin. It does not re-profile management (agent 01), re-score earnings quality (earnings/06, cross-referenced), value the company, or rate the stock.*

Cross-module inputs used: `01_management-and-track-record.md` (the promise-vs-delivery record), `earnings/06_earnings-quality.md` (non-GAAP aggressiveness, accruals), `earnings/04_guidance-consensus.md` (guidance reliability / beat-miss), and `earnings/08_earnings-red-flags.md` (narrative-vs-numbers flags, the auditor edit-log finding). All were available — **no partial-data cap applies.** Candor is assessed from two full bad-news transcripts plus the audited filings, so the read is not filings-only.

**Context that frames this read:** HCG is private-equity-controlled (KKR took sole control 30-May-2025), the CEO (Mattoo) has ~11 months in seat, and the third CFO in ~33 months joined end-May-2026 [01 §1–2]. So the communication pattern below is largely the **new KKR-installed team's emerging style** (two earnings calls and one strategy meet), while the multi-year *bottom-line* miss it has to explain spans the prior CVC-era team too. There are no pre-changeover (CVC-era) transcripts or a separate CEO shareholder letter in the pool — the candor lens is the current team's calls plus the FY24-25 / FY23-24 audited Annual Reports.

---

#### 1. Owning the Misses

The misses to assess (from `01` §4 and `earnings/04` §6): the company hits revenue and EBITDA but **misses the bottom line** — normalized EPS missed consensus in 3 of the last 4 fiscal years (FY23 −19.2%, FY25 −24.5%, FY26 −8.9%; only FY24 beat), and FY26 reported PAT collapsed to ₹138 Mn from ₹444 Mn (FY25) and ₹482 Mn (FY24) even as EBITDA grew 20.3%. The worst-news period is therefore the FY26 result (PAT −69% YoY), discussed on the 20-May-2026 Q4/FY26 call; the prior bad-news quarter is Q3 FY26 (the Andhra Pradesh state-scheme strike), discussed on the 09-Feb-2026 call.

| Miss (from 01 / earnings) | How It Was Communicated | Owned or Obscured? | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| **FY26 reported PAT collapse to ₹138 Mn (−69% YoY)** — the single biggest miss | CEO's entire Q4/FY26 prepared remarks led with "profitable broad-based growth," clinical case studies (robotic surgery, a centenarian's immuno-radiation), adjusted EBITDA (+19%) and ROCE — and **never once stated reported PAT or that it fell**. The −69% decline appears only in the deck reconciliation, not the narrative. No analyst raised it; all opened with "congratulations on a good set of numbers." | **Obscured (by omission).** The number is disclosed in the deck (transparent), but the worst result of the year is absent from how management *framed* the year. Owning a miss means naming it; this named everything except it. | Q4/FY26 transcript, prepared remarks (20-May-2026), p.2–4; reconciliation in `earnings/06` §7; `Q4&FY26 deck, p.20` |
| **Recurring below-EBITDA EPS miss (3 of 4 FYs)** | Never characterised as a miss on any call. The Q4 framing was that FY26 *improved* "quality of earnings" and "stronger cash generation." The structural reason the bottom line misses (D&A, finance cost, goodwill-heavy M&A, exceptionals) is not volunteered by management; it is reconstructed by the earnings module. | **Obscured.** A four-year pattern of bottom-line misses is not acknowledged; the narrative stays at the line (EBITDA) where the record is good. | `earnings/04` §6; `earnings/08` §2.10 ("'earnings accelerating' true only at EBITDA; PAT down" — High) |
| **Q4 FY26 revenue +11.3% vs the ~15% trend** (operating-line softness) | Asked directly in the first question. CEO answered specifically and without deflection: conscious paring of low-margin business (flagged "for a couple of months now") + Middle-East-conflict drag on medical-value travel; named the new International Sales head working it; said it "normalize[s] over the next couple of quarters." | **Owned (operating line).** A specific, falsifiable, non-defensive answer — the better candor moment in the file. The "normalises" claim is a forward judgement, not yet data [`earnings/08` §2.10]. | Q4/FY26 transcript, Q&A lines 192–205 (20-May-2026) |
| **Q3 FY26 Andhra Pradesh state-scheme strike** (volume hit) | Disclosed proactively in prepared remarks: "temporary disruptions in Andhra Pradesh… impacted volumes… resolved within the quarter." Gave the ex-AP growth figure (South 11% ex-AP vs 9% reported) and quantified the strike (~20–25 working days) when asked. Odisha scheme transition also disclosed with its −3% ARPP impact. | **Owned.** Bad operating news disclosed before being asked, with a clean like-for-like figure. A genuine candor positive. | Q3/9M FY26 transcript, prepared remarks lines 166–181, Q&A lines 411–420 (09-Feb-2026) |
| **FY26 CFO/EBITDA conversion fell to ~75%** (from 82–86% trend) [`earnings/06` §2] | Asked directly whether there were one-offs. CEO immediately reached for the adjusted figure: "when you adjust for ESOPs settlement… the CFO is actually 87%. So that is the one-off." | **Mixed — walked up, not owned.** The reflex is to restate the number higher via an adjustment rather than discuss the multi-year erosion (84%→74.5%) and the working-capital drain behind it. | Q4/FY26 transcript, Q&A lines 383–387 (20-May-2026); erosion trend `earnings/06` §2 |
| **Rights-issue rationale** (₹425 cr equity raised despite leverage already ~1.5x) | An analyst pushed hard on Q3 (Feb-2026): leverage is low, why raise equity? CEO: "an equity infusion at this stage will strengthen the company's balance sheet… that is all I can divulge. But very shortly, we will share the specifics after our Board meets." | **Obscured / deflected** at the time. The deleveraging "promise" in `01` was kept via this equity raise, not FCF [01 §4]; here management would not explain the rationale beyond generalities on a material capital-allocation question. | Q3/9M FY26 transcript, Q&A lines 305–321 (09-Feb-2026) |
| **Founder contract amendment (₹2 cr one-time)** | Disclosed; analyst told the timing directly (hits Q4, not Q3). | **Owned.** A small related-party/governance item handled plainly. | Q3/9M FY26 transcript, Q&A lines 485–491 (09-Feb-2026) |

**Read:** the pattern is asymmetric. *Operating* bad news (Q4 revenue dip, the AP strike, the Odisha scheme) is owned — disclosed proactively or answered specifically. The *bottom-line* miss — the PAT collapse and the four-year EPS-miss pattern, which is the most important miss in the file — is **not owned**; it is omitted from the narrative and left to the deck's (transparent) reconciliation, while the framing stays on the EBITDA line where the record is good.

#### 2. Non-GAAP / Adjustment Aggressiveness

Cross-checked against `earnings/06` §4–7 and `earnings/08` §2.7. HCG discloses Adjusted EBITDA (adds back ESOP + "one-time cost") and Adjusted PAT (adds back two exceptionals). It does **not** publish adjusted EBIT or adjusted EPS.

| Signal | Detail | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Recurring "one-off" addbacks | **Two recur and are flagged.** (1) **ESOP** is added back to Adjusted EBITDA *every year* (₹78.5 / ₹65.4 / ₹14.4 Mn FY24–FY26) — a recurring real cost excluded in principle, though now tiny (0.06% of FY26 revenue). (2) The **labour-code provision (₹127 Mn)** added back to reach Adjusted PAT is a **recurring forward statutory cost (gratuity/PF under the new Labour Codes) presented as a one-off normalisation** — the cash will be paid once the codes are in force. So the "clean" adjusted PAT is itself flattered. | `earnings/06` §4–5; `earnings/08` §2.7 ("labour-code 'one-off' is a recurring forward cost" — Medium) |
| GAAP-to-adjusted gap (magnitude / trend) | **At EBITDA: trivial — +1.15%** (₹4,658 Mn reported → ₹4,711 Mn adjusted). **At PAT: enormous — +304.7%** (₹138 Mn reported → ₹557 Mn adjusted). The PAT gap is driven by ₹319 Mn goodwill impairment (genuine, Milann-disposal-related, non-cash, one-off) + the ₹127 Mn labour-code item (recurring, mislabeled). Both sum exactly to the ₹445.8 Mn audited "Extraordinary Items" line (319.1 + 126.7 = 445.8 ✓). | `earnings/06` §7; `Q4&FY26 deck, p.8, p.20` |
| Does adjusted framing flatter reality? | **At EBITDA, no** — the adjustment is immaterial and the construct is honest (management's "~75% EBITDA-to-CFO" is even slightly conservative vs adjusted EBITDA). **At PAT, partly yes** — management leads on adjusted EBITDA/PAT and never states reported PAT on the call, and one of the two PAT add-backs recurs, so the headline "clean" figure (₹557 Mn) overstates the durable run-rate. Crucially, the *reconciliation itself is fully disclosed and the items are real* — this is **transparent flattery, not deception**: a reader who opens the deck can see exactly what was added back. | `earnings/06` §7, §9–10; Q4/FY26 transcript prepared remarks |

**Non-GAAP verdict: mostly disciplined at EBITDA, mildly aggressive at PAT.** The EBITDA construct is clean; the aggressiveness is (a) leading the entire narrative on adjusted figures while omitting reported PAT, and (b) treating one recurring statutory cost as a one-off. Because every adjustment is disclosed and reconciles to the audited line, this is a framing/emphasis issue, not a fabrication issue. *Net: lean toward "aggressive" on emphasis, "conservative/clean" on substance at EBITDA.*

#### 3. Disclosure Transparency

Disclosure **got less transparent over time on the operating KPIs that matter, under the new team — a warning.** HCG dropped occupancy / ARPOB (bed-utilisation) reporting and replaced it with a company-defined, blended **"center utilisation" (58%)** metric; when an analyst explicitly asked them to restore the bed/occupancy metric ("with the change in reporting format, we have done away with bed or occupancy-related metric… help us add that"), the CEO declined and said they would "stick to" volume and ARPP [Q3/9M FY26 transcript, Q&A lines 442–450; Q4/FY26 transcript lines 264, 353–356]. Management also **refused to disclose center-wise economics** ("refraining from giving center-wise details") and **declined to quantify the low-margin revenue it foregone** ("Right now we may not be able to answer that question"), the one number an analyst needed for a like-for-like read [Q4/FY26 transcript lines 268–272, 406–409]. Separately, a disclosure that sits in the filing but is **omitted from management's "clean audit" framing**: the FY25 auditor's Rule 11(g) report states the audit-trail (edit-log) feature "was not enabled in the accounting software used" for certain revenue/consumption and payroll-master books (no tampering found) — a control-design gap on exactly the books where it matters [FY24-25 AR, Independent Auditor's Report, Rule 11(g), print p.219–220; `earnings/08` §2.7]. On the positive side, segment disclosure is structurally simple (single oncology segment, >97% of revenue), the cluster-level revenue/growth/volume split *is* given each quarter, and the Milann-divestiture economics were disclosed in full (EV ₹63 cr, equity ₹37.6 cr, 75% upfront / 25% over 18 months) [Q4/FY26 transcript lines 124–131]. Net: cluster and transaction disclosure is adequate; the **granular operating KPI that lets outsiders check capacity utilisation was removed under the current team and not restored on request** — the single clearest transparency warning here.

#### 4. Tone in Bad Times

The tone in the worst recent periods is **promotional, not candid in emphasis** — though with genuine candor on operating specifics. Both bad-news calls (Q3 FY26's AP strike; Q4 FY26's PAT collapse) opened with extended clinical-showcase monologues (a tumour "encircling the optic nerve," a 97-year-old's robotic resection, a centenarian's immuno-radiation) and "profitable broad-based growth" language before reaching the numbers, and **neither call's prepared remarks stated reported PAT**, the year's worst figure [Q4/FY26 transcript p.2–4; Q3/9M FY26 transcript p.1–3]. Q&A is answered directly on most operating questions (the Q4 revenue dip, the AP strike days, receivables, margin-bucket moves) — the receivables answer even gave hard figures (Q3 billing ~₹25 cr, net receivable benefit ~₹5 cr) [Q3 transcript lines 472–484] — but it deflects on the harder governance/capital questions: the rights-issue rationale ("that is all I can divulge"), center-wise detail, and the quantum of foregone low-margin revenue [Q3 lines 318–321; Q4 lines 268–272, 406–409]. So: candid on *what happened operationally*, promotional on *how the year is framed*, and evasive on a few specific items where a straight answer would expose a weaker point.

#### 5. Candor Read

**Verdict: mixed.** Management can be trusted to disclose *operating* bad news plainly — the Andhra Pradesh scheme strike was volunteered with a clean ex-AP growth figure, and the Q4 revenue miss was answered specifically — and every adjustment is disclosed and reconciles exactly to the audited "Extraordinary Items" line, so this is not deception. But it cannot yet be trusted to *own the bottom-line miss that matters*: the FY26 reported PAT collapse (−69% YoY) and the four-year normalized-EPS miss pattern are absent from the narrative on both bad-news calls, the entire framing leads on adjusted EBITDA/PAT while never stating reported PAT, one recurring statutory cost (labour-code) is dressed as a one-off inside that adjusted figure, and — the single biggest candor signal — the new team **removed the occupancy/bed-utilisation KPI and refused to restore it on a direct analyst request**, replacing it with a softer company-defined metric, while also declining to quantify the low-margin revenue it foregone. The strongest evidence *for* candor is the proactive AP-strike disclosure and the specific receivables answer; the strongest evidence *against* is the disclosure downgrade on operating KPIs plus the omission of reported PAT from how the worst year was sold. This is a private-equity-controlled company with a ~11-month-old CEO, so the read is a provisional one on an emerging communication pattern, and it lands as **mixed** — better than promotional/low-trust because the substance is disclosed and the audit is unmodified, worse than candid/high-trust because the worst news is obscured by emphasis and disclosure granularity was reduced under the current team.

#### Disclosure Candor Score

*Higher = better. Score reflects: operating bad news is owned and disclosures are SEBI-LODR-compliant and reconcile to audited lines (pulls up); the worst (bottom-line) miss is obscured by emphasis, one recurring cost is dressed as a one-off, and an operating KPI was made less transparent under the new team (pulls down). No partial-data cap (two full bad-news transcripts + guidance/earnings-quality cross-checks all present).*

| Component | Score | Max Score | Evidence |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| Owning misses | 11 | 25 | Operating misses owned — AP strike volunteered with ex-AP figure [06-004], Q4 revenue dip answered specifically [06-003], founder-contract item disclosed. But the *largest* miss is obscured: FY26 reported PAT (−69%) omitted from both bad-news narratives [06-001, 06-015] and the four-year EPS-miss pattern never acknowledged [06-002]; CFO-conversion fall restated up rather than owned [06-005]; rights-issue rationale deflected [06-006]. Net below midpoint — operating candor good, bottom-line candor weak. |
| KPI consistency | 8 | 20 | **Disclosure downgrade under the current team:** occupancy/ARPOB dropped and replaced with a company-defined "center utilisation," and not restored on a direct analyst request [06-011, RF-MGT-003]; center-wise economics and foregone-revenue quantum withheld [06-012]. The cluster revenue/growth/volume split is consistent quarter-to-quarter (partial offset). A measurable reduction in granularity → low score. |
| Non-GAAP / adjustment discipline | 13 | 20 | EBITDA construct clean (+1.15%, honest) [06-007]. Held down by the +305% adjusted-vs-reported PAT gap led on while reported PAT is omitted [06-008], one recurring statutory cost (labour-code) mislabeled as a one-off [06-009, RF-DISC-002], and ESOP added back every year [06-010]. Disciplined at EBITDA, mildly aggressive at PAT — but fully disclosed/reconciling, so not deception. |
| Q&A transparency | 9 | 15 | Direct and specific on most operating questions (revenue dip, AP strike days, receivables with hard figures, margin-bucket moves) [06-003, 06-004, 06-016]; deflects on rights-issue rationale, center-wise detail, and foregone low-margin revenue [06-006, 06-012, 06-016]. Mixed. |
| Disclosure timeliness | 9 | 10 | All results, transcripts, the control change, KMP changes, the Milann sale and the founder-contract amendment filed on time under SEBI-LODR Reg 30/Schedule III; no delayed-results or delayed-material-disclosure flag (RF-REG-002 not triggered) [01 §2; disqualifier scan §1]. |
| Specificity vs boilerplate | 5 | 10 | Operating answers are specific (per-cluster growth, bed counts, ₹/bed capex, receivable flows). But prepared remarks lean heavily on clinical-showcase and "profitable broad-based growth" framing, and the worst number is absent [06-015]; several hard items met "we may not be able to answer that" [06-012]. Below midpoint. |
| Total | 55 | 100 | **Mixed band (41–60, "average").** Compliant, reconciling, operationally candid disclosure (pulls up); the worst miss obscured by emphasis, a recurring cost dressed as a one-off, and a KPI made less transparent under the new team (pulls down). |

**Disclosure candor /100: 55 — "mixed."** Maps to the constitution's 41–60 "mixed/average" band. The score is held below 60 by three things working together: the FY26 reported-PAT collapse omitted from how the worst year was framed, the labour-code recurring cost inside the "adjusted" figure, and the reduction in operating-KPI disclosure under the current team. It clears 50 because the adjustments are fully disclosed and reconcile to the audited line, the audit is unmodified, operating bad news (the AP strike, the Q4 dip) is owned, and all disclosures are filed on time.

# PART VI — Valuation · FULL MODULE (synthesis + 8 specialist reports)

*Folder: `analyses/HCG_2026-06-01/valuation/`. The module synthesis appears first (the module's own adjudicated verdict), followed by each specialist report in pipeline order, reproduced in full.*

## VAL-99 · Module Synthesis — Valuation Module — HCG (Synthesis)

### Valuation Module — HCG (Synthesis)

#### Abstract

HealthCare Global Enterprises (NSE: HCG) is modestly overvalued: at ₹646.15 the price sits ~11% above a base-case fair value of ~₹575 and ~32% above a defensible bear. The fair-value range is ≈₹400 (bear) / ~₹575 (base, ₹520–₹620) / ~₹735 (bull), driven by the relative/peer method, which shows HCG's −32% EV/EBITDA "discount" evaporating into a +9% EV/EBIT and +64% NTM P/E premium once weak profit conversion is priced. At ₹646.15 the market prices ~33% free-cash-flow growth for a decade — a rate HCG has never produced (FCF fell from ₹1,184M to ₹586M over FY23–FY26). There is no margin of safety (−12.4%); ~32% of downside exists to the bear. Verdict: modestly overvalued with explicit value-trap risk.

#### 1. Valuation Verdict

- **Verdict:** **Modestly overvalued** *(base-case fair value ~₹575 sits ~11% below the ₹646.15 price; three of four value-producing methods and the reverse-DCF land at-to-below price)*
- **Base-case fair-value range (per share):** **₹520–₹620 (midpoint ~₹575)** *(from 07)*
- **Current price:** **₹646.15** *(NSE last close, ~late-May 2026 pool snapshot; from 01)*
- **Bull / Base / Bear fair-value levels:** **Bull ₹700–₹770 (~₹735) / Base ₹520–₹620 (~₹575) / Bear ₹400–₹480 (~₹440)** *(from 07)*
- Valuation attractiveness /100 *(higher = cheaper)*: **34** *(price ~11% above base FV; negative upside to base, ~32% downside to bear — weak, not washed-out)*
- Margin of safety /100 *(higher = better)*: **25** *(MoS = −12.4%; price above base fair value, no downside protection; assessable because a current price is present)*
- Valuation confidence /100: **52** *(four independent methods ran and a price exists, but DCF is terminal-dominated (TV 67–90% of EV) and the defensible-method spread exceeds 40% — capped at 55 per MODULE_RULES; held at 52 because the cross-method gap is large even if reconciled as methodological)*
- Downside risk /100 *(higher = WORSE — inverted)*: **64** *(~32% to the central bear ₹440; deep-stress DCF cash-flow floor ≈₹142 if growth is judged permanently value-dilutive; high-fixed-cost operating deleverage makes the downside non-linear)*
- Data quality /100: **80** *(from 00: full IS/CF/BS, forward consensus, own-history + forward multiples, 10-peer comp set, and a current price all present; minor gaps — two price snapshots ~1.4% apart, WACC inputs web-sourced, FY26 on audited-deck/CIQ-PR grade not full statutory statements)*
- Overall usefulness /100: **78** *(all five methods evaluable; clear, reconciled fair-value range and a sharp what's-priced-in read; no usefulness cap binds — single-segment SOTP collapse is structural, not a data gap)*
- Dominant valuation method: **Relative/peer multiples (03)** — for a single-segment hospital operator priced by the market on EV/EBITDA, the peer set is the only method that isolates HCG's actual weakness (the EBITDA discount collapses to a premium at EV/EBIT and P/E); the DCF corroborates the *direction* (ROIC < WACC) but its ₹90–767 width cannot set the level.
- What's priced in: **~33% FCF CAGR for a decade on a normalized base (~42% on literal FY26 FCF), fading to 5% — a rate HCG has never produced; FCF has *fallen* over FY23–FY26** *(from 05)*.
- Biggest valuation risk: **Value trap** — buying the headline −32% EV/EBITDA discount to peers as if it were upside, when it is the market correctly paying less per rupee of HCG EBITDA because HCG converts EBITDA into EBIT and PAT far worse (D&A ₹2,442M, finance cost ₹1,766M, sub-cost-of-capital ROIC for six years).

#### 2. Specialist Roll-Up

| Specialist | Verdict Line | Biggest Finding |
|---|---|---|
| valuation-data-triage | Sufficient — four independent methods can run; no partial-data cap binds | A current price IS present (₹646.15 / ₹637.25); single-segment SOTP collapse is structural, not a data gap, so no usefulness cap |
| price-and-capital-structure | Anchor set: price ₹646.15, market cap ₹96,461.1M, EV ₹109,187.9M, net debt ₹11,944.4M, 151.79M fully diluted | Leases ₹8,249.5M sit inside debt/EV (Ind AS 116) → EV/EBITDA must stay post-lease; rights issue lifted shares to 149.30M and reset book value per share to ~₹88 |
| multiples-own-history | Mid-to-low range on operating multiples; equity-multiple "lows" are mechanical, not a quality re-rating | No clean multi-year mean to revert to — 6-quarter window says +2–3%, the 4-year window says −12% to −23%; window-dependence is the finding |
| relative-valuation-peers | Roughly fairly-valued-to-modestly-rich vs peers once quality is priced; warranted ₹491–714 (mid ~₹603, −7%) | The −32% EV/EBITDA discount is warranted and **flips to a +9% EV/EBIT and +64% NTM P/E premium** — explicit value-trap risk |
| intrinsic-dcf | Cash-flow lens ₹90–190 (base ₹142); multiple-persistence lens ₹481–767; combined ₹90–767; confidence Low | ROIC ~4.5–5.0% vs WACC ~10.7% makes reinvested growth value-dilutive; a disciplined Gordon DCF cannot reach the price at any sane terminal growth |
| reverse-dcf | Price implies ~33% FCF CAGR for a decade — aggressive | HCG's FCF has *declined* (₹1,184M → ₹586M, FY23–FY26); the implied bar is something HCG has never done; points to downside, not embedded upside |
| sum-of-the-parts | SOTP collapses — single-segment (~98.5% of Adj. EBITDA from oncology network); base ~₹530 (−18%) | The EV/EBITDA discount is not a hidden segment waiting to be unlocked; the value lever is internal centre-maturity, already inside consolidated EBITDA |
| scenario-and-fair-value | Base ~₹575 (₹520–₹620); no margin of safety (−12.4%); ~32% to bear | On every method that prices HCG the way the market prices hospitals, fair value clusters ₹530–₹645 — at-to-below price; only an unwarranted multiple reaches above |

#### 3. Reconciliation

The methods **agree on the conclusion and disagree on the magnitude**. Three of four value-producing methods (peers ~₹603, SOTP ~₹530, DCF cash-flow ~₹142) and the reverse-DCF all sit below the ₹646.15 price; only own-history's 6-quarter EV-multiple reversion (₹624–667) sits at the price.

The high-to-low spread across the *defensible* method outputs is large — ₹142 (DCF financeable-Gordon) to ₹714 (peer warranted high), a >5x range that trips the >40% cross-method flag. This disagreement is **methodological, not informational**, and `07` reconciled rather than averaged it: the DCF cash-flow low and the multiple-based highs answer two different questions — "what cash can this business distribute if it must fund its own growth at a ~5% ROIC" (answer: little, because ROIC < WACC) versus "what will a buyer pay for hospital EBITDA at 15–25x" (answer: a lot, regardless of ROIC). Both are legitimate; they measure different things.

The reconciled view: the **multiple-based cluster (₹530–₹645) is the base case**, the **DCF cash-flow low (~₹142–₹190) is the deep-stress floor / margin-of-safety anchor**, and nothing supports a value comfortably above the quote without assuming a multiple HCG's returns do not warrant. I trust the **peer/relative method most** for HCG because it is the only one that exposes the profit-conversion gap a naive EBITDA-only read would miss. The DCF's >40% internal divergence and terminal dominance are why valuation confidence is capped (Section 4), not why the verdict changes — the directional signal is consistent across every method.

#### 4. Score Cap Application

| Cap Trigger | Applied? (Y/N) | Affected Score | Final Cap |
|---|---|---|---|
| No current price | **N** | MoS / attractiveness | Price IS present (₹646.15) — MoS is assessable; no cap |
| No consensus / forward estimates | **N** | Valuation confidence | Consensus present (FY26–FY29 EPS/Rev/EBITDA, target price, forward multiples) — no cap |
| No peer data | **N** | Overall usefulness | 10 named peers present — no cap |
| Only one valuation method usable | **N** | Valuation confidence | Four independent methods ran — no cap |
| No cash flow AND DCF is only method | **N** | Valuation confidence | Full cash flow statement present; DCF is not the only method — no cap |
| SOTP not possible for multi-segment | **N** | Overall usefulness | Single-segment is structural (one Ind AS 108 segment, ~98.5% of Adj. EBITDA), not a data gap — cap does not apply |
| Methods disagree >40% unreconciled | **Y (reconciled)** | Valuation confidence | Defensible spread ₹142–₹714 exceeds 40%; `07` reconciled it as methodological → confidence max **55** |
| Terminal value >75% of DCF EV | **Y** | Valuation confidence | DCF terminal value is 67% (Method A) to 86–90% (Method B) of EV → confidence max **60** |

Most restrictive applicable cap on valuation confidence: **55** (methods-disagree gate, reconciled). Confidence is set at **52**, inside the cap, reflecting that the cross-method gap is real even though it is methodological. No cap binds margin of safety or usefulness.

#### 5. Fair-Value Summary

Fair value is **≈₹400 (bear) / ~₹575 (base, ₹520–₹620) / ~₹735 (bull)**, and the single method that drives it is the **peer/relative read**: it shows the −32% EV/EBITDA discount to listed multi-specialty peers is warranted and flips to a +9% EV/EBIT and +64% NTM P/E premium the moment you reach profit, because HCG converts a rupee of EBITDA into EBIT and PAT far worse than peers (D&A ₹2,442M, finance cost ₹1,766M, net margin ~0.5% vs ~12% peer median). The current price of ₹646.15 implies ~33% free-cash-flow growth for a decade on a normalized base (~42% on literal FY26 FCF) — and the evidence says that is not achievable: HCG's FCF has *fallen* from ₹1,184M to ₹586M over FY23–FY26, ROIC has been 4.5–5.0% against a ~10.7–12.4% cost of capital in every one of the last six years, and the moat is narrow and economically unproven. The margin of safety is negative (−12.4%): the price sits above base fair value, with ~32% of downside to the central bear (~₹440) and a deep-stress DCF cash-flow floor near ₹142 if growth is judged permanently value-dilutive. **This is an explicit value-trap risk** — the cheap-looking EBITDA multiple is one the business does not deserve to re-rate from until ramping centres push returns above the cost of capital, which `business-model/09` flags as unproven and `earnings/04` shows the Street still cutting (FY27 EPS-N down −19% over six months).

#### 6. What Would Change The Valuation Verdict?

| Current Verdict | What Would Make It Cheaper | What Would Make It More Expensive | Data Needed |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Modestly overvalued** (base ~₹575 vs price ₹646.15) | Evidence the cohort-maturity inflection is converting: ramping centres migrating into the >₹100M/month, ~26%-margin / 27%-ROCE mature bucket; group ROIC crossing the ~10.7–12.4% cost of capital; FCF conversion rising as capex falls below D&A; West cluster utilisation moving from ~50% toward South's ~68% — any of which would warrant a higher multiple and lift the base FV toward the bull band | A price fall toward the ₹520–₹530 base-low (restoring a margin of safety); a state-scheme disruption recurring or the West ramp stalling (compressing EBITDA via operating deleverage, pulling toward the ₹400–480 bear); the hospital sector de-rating from the current 22–35x EV/EBITDA band | Full statutory FY26 financials (pool has audited-deck/CIQ-PR grade only); quarterly centre-level utilisation/ROCE disclosure to test the maturity migration; a name-specific (not vendor-distorted) beta to tighten WACC; a listed pure-play oncology comp (AOI is private; Apollo's vertical undisclosed) |

#### 7. Note To The Final Synthesizer

- **Fair-value range:** Bull ~₹735 (₹700–₹770) / Base ~₹575 (₹520–₹620) / Bear ~₹440 (₹400–₹480). Driven by the **peer/relative method**, which isolates HCG's profit-conversion gap; the DCF corroborates the direction (ROIC < WACC) but its ₹90–767 width cannot set the level alone.
- **What the price implies (reverse-DCF):** ~33% FCF CAGR for a decade (~42% on literal FY26 FCF). Not achievable on the record — FCF has *fallen* (₹1,184M → ₹586M, FY23–FY26); the price points to downside, not embedded upside.
- **Margin of safety / downside anchor:** **None** (−12.4%); price sits ~11% above base FV and ~32% above the central bear ~₹440. Deep-stress DCF cash-flow floor ~₹142 if growth is judged permanently value-dilutive.
- **Genuine value vs value trap:** **Value-trap risk** — the −32% EV/EBITDA discount is warranted, not opportunity; it flips to a +9% EV/EBIT and +64% NTM P/E *premium* once weak profit conversion is priced. Warranted multiple = a quality discount to the 34.6x peer median (NTM ~18–21x EV/EBITDA), not a re-rating; HCG does not warrant the profit-multiple premiums it already carries.
- **Which method to trust / discount for THIS company:** Trust **peers (03)** — it captures the profit-conversion gap. Use **DCF (04)** for its structural truth (ROIC < WACC) but discount its level (terminal-dominated, two terminal methods diverge >40%). Discount **own-history (02)** as a value-setter — no clean multi-year mean; its equity-multiple "lows" are mechanical (rights issue reset book). **SOTP (06)** is a single-segment sanity check, not an independent breakup.
- **Partial-data caps:** A current price IS present, so margin of safety is fully assessable and no "no price" cap applies. The only caps are on *valuation confidence* (methods-disagree-reconciled → max 55; terminal value >75% of DCF EV → max 60); set at 52. No usefulness or MoS cap binds.
- **Biggest missing data point:** Full statutory FY26 financial statements — the pool's FY26 is audited-deck/CIQ "Press Release" grade, not the complete audited statements; this is the single highest-value next data request, ahead of a name-specific beta and centre-level utilisation/ROCE disclosure.
- **Explicit handoff:** The master synthesizer's "Valuation and Peer Mispricing" section should **defer to this synthesis**. The bull/base/bear fair-value LEVELS here (₹735 / ₹575 / ₹440) are the inputs for the master's probability-weighted scenario model — **the master assigns the probabilities, computes the probability-weighted target and risk/reward, and issues the rating; this module does not.** The swing factor the master should weight is whether the 14 ramping centres complete the cohort-maturity migration and push ROIC above the cost of capital (bull) or stall into compounding downside drivers (bear).

#### 8. Simple Summary

- **Modestly overvalued.** Base fair value ~₹575 vs price ₹646.15 — the price is ~11% too high.
- Fair-value range: **₹400 (bear) / ~₹575 (base) / ~₹735 (bull)**.
- The market is pricing in **~33% FCF growth for a decade** — HCG has never done it; its FCF has actually been falling.
- Downside sits at **~₹440 (central bear), ~32% below today** — and a deep-stress cash-flow floor near ₹142.
- The **peer comparison matters most**: HCG looks cheap on EBITDA but is at a premium on EV/EBIT and P/E because it earns far less profit per rupee of revenue.
- **Value-trap risk is real** — the cheap-looking EBITDA multiple is deserved (low margins, high leverage, sub-cost-of-capital returns for six years), not a buy signal.
- A current price **was available** (₹646.15), so margin of safety is fully assessable — there is **none** (−12.4%).
- This module **is useful** for the master synthesizer: a clear, reconciled fair-value range and a sharp what's-priced-in read; confidence is capped (52) only because the DCF is terminal-dominated and the methods diverge in magnitude.

---

#### 6A. Method Readiness Matrix

| Method | Ready? (Y/N) | Blocking Missing Inputs | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Own-history multiples | Y | None | Financials.xls "Multiples" sheet has quarterly LTM/NTM TEV/EBITDA, TEV/EBIT, P/E, P/BV history; current-cap multiples also provided. Note P/E is "NM" in places (depressed/loss-affected net income) — anchor on EV/EBITDA |

| Intrinsic DCF (Operating FCFF) | Y | None | Operating company; full IS + CF + BS history (FY21–FY25 + FY26 PR), capex and ΔNWC disclosed, consensus forward path available. WACC components (risk-free, ERP, beta) are NOT in the pool — flag as web-sourced/assumption inputs per MODULE_RULES |
| Reverse DCF | Y | None | Current price present, so "what's priced in" is computable; hold DCF discount rate/horizon and solve for implied growth/margin |
| SOTP | N (collapses) | Single reported segment; no audited segment EBIT with matched peer multiples | Business is >85% one segment (oncology hospital network). `06` should state "single-segment — SOTP collapses to the consolidated read." The only meaningful internal split (centre-maturity cohorts: 4 mature at 27% ROCE vs 6 emerging at −22% ROCE; geography clusters; Milann fertility being divested) is deck-only and not a valuable segment SOTP. Do not fabricate segment multiples |

Five distinct methods are evaluable; four produce independent fair-value reads, and SOTP correctly collapses to consolidated. That is well above the two-method bar.

---

#### 6. Sufficiency Verdict

- **Verdict:** Sufficient
- **Reason:** A complete operating-company financial base (income statement, cash flow, balance sheet/capital structure), forward consensus estimates, own-history and forward multiples, a 10-peer comp set, AND a current price are all present in the pool, so at least four independent valuation methods can run.
- **Methods that can run:** own-history multiples; peer relative valuation; intrinsic FCFF DCF; reverse-DCF. SOTP is not a separate method here (single reported segment → collapses to the consolidated read).
- **Active partial-data caps:** None. (No MODULE_RULES.md score cap binds. The single-segment SOTP collapse is a structural feature, not a data gap.)
- **Critical missing items:** None that block valuation. Items downstream agents must handle explicitly, but which do NOT change the Sufficient verdict:
  - Two current-price figures exist with different as-of dates (CIQ Current Cap ₹637.25 vs last close ₹646.15 NSE / ₹648.10 BSE) — `01` must select one, date it, and reuse it verbatim as the anchor.
  - Share-count figures vary across sheets (diluted WA 141.49M FY25 / 143.33M FY26 PR; Current Cap 149.30M; filing-date 144.67M) — `01` must reconcile market-cap count vs per-share fair-value (fully diluted) count.
  - WACC inputs (risk-free rate, equity-risk premium, beta) are not in the pool — `04`/`05` must supply them as dated web-sourced/assumption inputs and flag accordingly.
  - FY26 is the latest period but its source is the investor deck / earnings transcript / CIQ "Press Release" column, not yet the audited FY26 annual report (not in pool) — adjusted-vs-reported and the ₹319 Mn fertility goodwill impairment must be handled by `06`-earnings-quality guidance.

## VAL-01 · price-and-capital-structure — Price & Capital Structure — HCG

### Price & Capital Structure — HCG

**Company:** HealthCare Global Enterprises Limited (NSE: HCG, BSE: 539787) — pan-India oncology-focused hospital network.
**Reporting currency:** Indian Rupee (INR, ₹). All amounts in ₹ million (₹ Mn) unless a per-share or bn figure is stated. Fiscal year ends 31 March; FY26 = year ended 31 Mar 2026.
**Date:** 2026-06-01.
**Business type (from `00_valuation-data-triage`):** Operating company (Health Care Facilities). The EV bridge below is a value-relevant input (operating-company method map), not informational-only.
**Anchor source basis:** Capital IQ exports in the data pool are the primary price/capital-structure source (no IBKR screenshot in pool). The FY26 capital-structure figures are independently corroborated against the company's own audited FY26 results deck (board-approved, filed 19 May 2026), so they sit at filing-grade quality, not vendor-only.

> **Cross-module inputs used:** `earnings/01_historical-financials.md` (FY26 levels: Reported EBITDA ₹4,658M, company net-debt definitions, rights-issue context) and `valuation/00_valuation-data-triage.md` (source map; flagged the two price figures and the share-count spread to reconcile here). Both were available and consumed rather than re-derived.

---

#### 1. Current Price

| Field | Value | Source | As-of Date |
|---|---|---|---|
| Current price (anchor) | ₹646.15 | EstimatesReport.xls "Latest Price/Last Close Price" (NSEI:HCG), conversion "Today's Spot Rate", current FY = Mar-31-2027 | ~late-May 2026 (most recent pool snapshot) |
| Alternate price (used by CIQ for its TEV) | ₹637.25 | Financials.xls "Current Capitalization" → Share Price; CIQ tearsheet (`HCG_CIQReportLandscape.rtf`) | basis tied to Mar-31-2026 capitalization snapshot |
| BSE last close (reference) | ₹648.10 | EstimatesReport.xls "Last Close Price" (BSE:539787) | ~late-May 2026 |
| Currency | INR (₹) | both exports | — |
| Price basis | Last close (exchange close, primary class NSE) | EstimatesReport.xls | — |
| 52-week range (context) | ₹513.30 – ₹804.65 (NSE) | EstimatesReport.xls "52 Wk. High/Low" | trailing 52 wk |

**Price reconciliation (required by triage).** Two pool prices exist and differ by ~1.4%: CIQ Current-Capitalization ₹637.25 (tied to a Mar-31-2026 snapshot) and the consensus export's NSE last close ₹646.15 (a later, "Today's Spot Rate" snapshot, current fiscal year already rolled to FY27). I take **₹646.15 (NSE last close)** as the headline anchor price because it is the more recent of the two pool figures and is exchange-close on the primary listing. Both are pool-sourced (Capital IQ); no web quote was needed, so the indicative/unverified label does not apply.

**Important downstream note on the market-cap price.** CIQ's pre-computed market cap and TEV (Section 3–4) are built on its own ₹637.25. Where this report shows CIQ's reproduced TEV, it uses ₹637.25 so the tie-out holds; where it states the canonical market cap for downstream agents, it uses the anchor ₹646.15. The two market-cap figures differ by ~₹1,440M (~1.5%) — small, but downstream agents must use one consistently. **Use ₹646.15 and the 149.30M share count (below).**

---

#### 2. Share Count

| Field | Value | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Basic shares outstanding (current, post rights issue) | 149.30M | Financials.xls "Current Capitalization" → Shares Out. 149.302203M; equals paid-up equity capital ₹1,493.0M ÷ ₹10 face value (Balance Sheet, Common Stock, FY26 PR col) |
| CIQ "Total Shares Out. on Filing Date" (FY26) | 144.67M | Financials.xls Balance Sheet, Supplemental, Filing Date serial 46161 (≈2026-05-15) — **anomalous, not used** (see note) |
| Diluted weighted-average shares (FY26) | 143.33M | Financials.xls Income Statement → Weighted Avg. Diluted Shares Out. (FY26 PR col) |
| Basic weighted-average shares (FY26) | 141.86M | Financials.xls Income Statement → Weighted Avg. Basic Shares Out. (FY26 PR col) |
| Options/RSUs outstanding | 3.22M @ W/avg strike ₹145.55 | Financials.xls Supplemental → Options Out. at end of period (FY25; FY26 not refreshed in export — dash) |
| Convertibles / potential shares | None | Financials.xls Capital Structure Details → "Convertible = No" on every debt line (FY24, FY25) |
| **Fully diluted shares (TSM, if-converted)** | **151.79M** | 149.30M basic + 2.48M net new from options via treasury-stock method (calc below); no converts |
| Share count used for **market cap** | 149.30M | most recent shares outstanding (paid-up capital basis) — per Fully-Diluted Equity Rule 1 |
| Share count used for **per-share fair value** | 151.79M (fully diluted, TSM) | per Fully-Diluted Equity Rule 2; falls back to nothing — full TSM applied |

**Share-count reconciliation (which count, why).**

- **149.30M is the correct current count.** Three independent ties confirm it: (1) CIQ Current Cap "Shares Out." = 149.302203M; (2) FY26 paid-up equity capital ₹1,493.0M ÷ ₹10 face value = 149.30M (Balance Sheet "Common Stock", FY26 PR col); (3) institutional ownership block of 29,356,888 shares = 19.66% of total implies 29,356,888 ÷ 0.1966 = 149.32M total (`Public Ownership Summary.rtf`). The ~10M jump from FY25 (139.42M) to FY26 (149.30M) is the **₹4,250 Mn rights issue completed in Q4 FY26, oversubscribed 1.3x** [HCG Q4 & FY26 Results deck, filed 19 May 2026, p.14].
- **The 144.67M "filing-date" field is rejected.** CIQ's Balance-Sheet supplemental field "Total Shares Out. on Filing Date" = 144.666666M does not reconcile with the company's own paid-up capital (it would imply a ₹10.32 face value, inconsistent with the ₹10 face on every prior year). It is a stale/interim CIQ proprietary field; tellingly, CIQ's own market-cap divisor is 149.30M, not 144.67M. Note that CIQ's printed Book Value/Share ₹92.07 and TBV/Share ₹63.01 were computed on this 144.67M figure — Section 6 recomputes them on 149.30M.
- **Material gap basic vs diluted WA:** diluted WA (143.33M) is the FY26 *period average* and predates full settlement of the rights issue, so it is below the current 149.30M post-issue count. For point-in-time market cap I use the current count (149.30M), not the period average — correct per the module's Fully-Diluted Equity Rule 1.

**Share Count Reconciliation Table (per MODULE_RULES Fully-Diluted Equity Rule 3):**

| Step | Shares (M) | Note |
|---|---:|---|
| Basic shares outstanding (current, post rights issue) | 149.30 | paid-up capital ₹1,493.0M ÷ ₹10 |
| + Options via treasury-stock method | +2.48 | 3.22M options − (3.22M × ₹145.55 ÷ ₹637.25 = 0.74M repurchased) = 2.48M net new |
| + Convertibles (if-converted) | +0.00 | none outstanding |
| **= Fully diluted shares used (per-share fair value)** | **151.79** | ~1.7% dilution over basic |

**TSM limitation:** Options-outstanding is the FY25 year-end count (3.22M, strike ₹145.55); the FY26 figure is not refreshed in the export. FY26 likely saw minor net option movement, so 151.79M fully diluted carries a small (<~1%) uncertainty. With the strike (₹145.55) deep in-the-money versus ₹637.25, the dilutive direction is settled even if the exact count drifts. This is the only dilution-data limitation; it does not force a fallback to diluted weighted-average.

---

#### 3. Market Capitalization

`Market cap = current shares outstanding × current price`

- **Canonical (anchor price ₹646.15 × 149.30M):** 646.15 × 149.302203 = **₹96,461.1M (₹96.46 bn)** — *use this downstream.*
- CIQ-reproduced (₹637.25 × 149.302203 = ₹95,142.8M, ₹95.14 bn) — shown only so the CIQ TEV tie-out in Section 4 reproduces exactly; not the downstream anchor.

The ₹1,318M difference between the two is purely the ₹637.25-vs-₹646.15 price gap on an identical share count. Downstream agents should use **₹96,461.1M** and note the ~1.4% price-snapshot sensitivity.

---

#### 4. Enterprise Value Bridge

All capital-structure components are as at 31 Mar 2026 (FY26), corroborated line-by-line against the company's audited FY26 results deck. Two EV figures are shown: the canonical bridge on the anchor price (₹646.15), and CIQ's reproduced TEV on ₹637.25 (tie-out check).

| Component | Amount (₹ Mn) | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Market capitalization (anchor, ₹646.15 × 149.30M) | 96,461.1 | Section 3 |
| + Total debt (short + long term, **incl. lease liabilities**) | 17,353.6 | Financials.xls Current Cap / Balance Sheet (FY26 PR); deck p.21 ties: non-curr borrowings 5,991.9 + non-curr leases 7,599.8 + curr borrowings 3,112.2 + curr leases 649.7 = 17,353.6 |
| + Minority / non-controlling interest | 782.4 | Financials.xls Current Cap; deck p.21 "Non-controlling interests 782.4" |
| + Preferred equity | 0.0 | Financials.xls Current Cap → "Pref. Equity = –" (none) |
| + Operating lease liabilities (separate add-on) | 0.0 (not added separately) | already inside Total Debt above (Ind AS 116 capitalized leases ₹8,249.5M); not double-counted |
| + Underfunded pension / other LT obligations | 0.0 (not added) | Pension & OPEB ₹252.6M is small (~0.2% of EV); CIQ excludes it from TEV — see "adjustments not made" |
| − Cash & equivalents (+ ST investments) | −5,409.2 | Financials.xls Current Cap / Balance Sheet (FY26 PR): Cash & Equivalents 5,409.2 (ST investments nil in FY26 col) |
| − Equity-method investments (treated separately) | 0.0 (not deducted) | Equity Method Investments ₹68.2M is immaterial (~0.06% of EV); left in — see "adjustments not made" |
| **= Enterprise value (EV), canonical (₹646.15)** | **109,187.9** | 96,461.1 + 17,353.6 + 782.4 + 0 − 5,409.2 |
| *Memo: EV reproduced on CIQ price (₹637.25)* | *107,869.6* | *ties exactly to CIQ "Total Enterprise Value (TEV)" 107,869.628861 ✓* |

**Bridge arithmetic shown:** EV = market cap + total debt + minority + preferred − cash. On the anchor price: 96,461.1 + 17,353.6 + 782.4 + 0.0 − 5,409.2 = **₹109,187.9M**. No plug. The CIQ-price memo line reproduces CIQ's published TEV to the rupee, confirming the bridge components are correct and only the price differs.

**Lease treatment — material, stated explicitly.** Total debt of ₹17,353.6M *includes* ₹8,249.5M of capitalized lease liabilities (Ind AS 116). This is ~47% of reported total debt and ~7.6% of EV. HCG is lease-heavy (hospital real estate). I keep leases inside debt to match CIQ's TEV and the CLAUDE.md net-debt default, but downstream EV/EBITDA users must note: HCG's EBITDA is post-Ind AS 116 (rent is below the line), so leaving leases in debt is the consistent treatment. An **EV excluding lease liabilities** would be **₹109,187.9 − 8,249.5 = ₹100,938.4M (anchor price)** — provided as a cross-check for any agent that prefers a pre-IFRS-16 / EV-to-EBITDAR frame. Pick one basis and hold it; do not mix lease-in-debt EV with pre-lease EBITDA.

**Adjustments deliberately NOT made (and why):**
- **Operating leases as a separate add-on:** not added — already capitalized inside total debt under Ind AS 116; adding CIQ's "Debt Equiv. of Oper. Leases" would double-count.
- **Underfunded pension / OPEB (₹252.6M):** not added to EV. Immaterial (~0.23% of EV) and CIQ excludes it from its TEV; flagged for completeness.
- **Equity-method investments (₹68.2M):** not deducted. Immaterial (~0.06% of EV); deducting would change EV by <0.1%.
- **Contingent liabilities / Milann fertility divestiture proceeds:** not adjusted. The Milann sale (consideration ~₹376M, close expected Q1 FY27) had not closed at 31 Mar 2026 [deck p.~250]; no pro-forma adjustment made — that is a forward event for later agents, not a balance-sheet fact today.
- **Margin-money bank balances / mutual-fund holdings:** CIQ classifies all ₹5,409.2M as cash; the company nets ~₹287M margin money and ~₹21M MF differently in its own net-debt schedule. I follow the CIQ/CLAUDE.md cash definition for the bridge and surface the company definition in Section 5.

---

#### 5. Net Debt & Leverage Snapshot

Net debt = total debt − cash & equivalents (CLAUDE.md / MODULE_RULES default). HCG also publishes its own net-debt definitions; both are shown so the gap is not hidden.

| Metric | Value | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Total debt (incl. leases) | ₹17,353.6M | Financials.xls Current Cap / Balance Sheet (FY26 PR) |
| Cash & equivalents (+ ST inv) | ₹5,409.2M | Financials.xls Current Cap / Balance Sheet (FY26 PR) |
| **Net debt (total debt − cash, incl. leases) — primary** | **₹11,944.4M** | derived; ties to CIQ Balance Sheet "Net Debt" FY26 field 11,944.4 ✓ |
| Net debt — company-defined, **incl. leases** (nets margin money + MF) | ₹11,636M | HCG Q4&FY26 deck, Net Debt schedule, 31 Mar 2026 |
| Net debt — company-defined, **excl. leases** | ₹3,387M | HCG Q4&FY26 deck, Net Debt schedule, 31 Mar 2026 |
| Lease liabilities (within total debt) | ₹8,249.5M | Balance Sheet FY26 PR (non-curr 7,599.8 + curr 649.7) |
| Net debt / FY26 Reported EBITDA (primary, incl. leases) | 2.56x | 11,944.4 ÷ 4,658 (Reported EBITDA, `earnings/01`; CIQ Key Stats EBITDA 4,657.9) |
| Total debt / FY26 Reported EBITDA | 3.73x | 17,353.6 ÷ 4,658 |
| Net debt (company ex-leases) / FY26 Reported EBITDA | 0.73x | 3,387 ÷ 4,658 (matches company-disclosed 0.73x) |

**Definition note (required):** the three net-debt lines differ for two reasons — (1) lease treatment (₹8,249.5M of Ind AS 116 leases in/out of debt), and (2) the company nets ~₹287M margin-money bank balances and ~₹21M mutual funds into cash that CIQ classifies elsewhere. Primary leverage on the CLAUDE.md default is **2.56x net debt / Reported EBITDA**; the company's headline **0.73x** is an ex-lease, ex-margin-money figure. EBITDA basis is **Reported** (GAAP/Ind AS, pre other-income), not adjusted; adjusted EBITDA (₹4,711M) would move the ratios trivially (2.54x / 0.72x). The Q4 FY26 rights issue (₹4,250M) is what cut net debt year-on-year, not free cash flow (FY26 FCF was ₹586M per `earnings/01`).

---

#### 6. Per-Share Reference Values

Per-share values use the **fully diluted** count (151.79M) per MODULE_RULES Fully-Diluted Equity Rule 2/4, except where noted. CIQ's printed per-share figures (BVPS ₹92.07, TBV/share ₹63.01) used 144.67M and are superseded.

| Metric | Per Share | Source / calc |
|---|---:|---|
| Book value per share (fully diluted) | ₹87.75 | Total Common Equity ₹13,319.7M (Balance Sheet FY26 PR) ÷ 151.79M |
| Book value per share (current basic 149.30M, memo) | ₹89.21 | 13,319.7 ÷ 149.30M |
| Tangible book value per share (fully diluted) | ₹60.05 | TBV ₹9,115.1M (= equity 13,319.7 − goodwill 3,875.9 − other intangibles 328.7; Balance Sheet FY26 PR) ÷ 151.79M |
| Tangible book value per share (basic 149.30M, memo) | ₹61.05 | 9,115.1 ÷ 149.30M |
| Net debt per share (primary, incl. leases) | −₹78.69 | net debt ₹11,944.4M ÷ 151.79M (negative = net DEBT, not net cash) |
| Net debt per share (company ex-leases) | −₹22.31 | ₹3,387M ÷ 151.79M |

HCG is a **net-debt** company (no net cash); the per-share net-debt figure is shown negative to make the sign explicit. Goodwill (₹3,875.9M) and other intangibles (₹328.7M) are ~32% of book equity, so tangible book is materially below book.

- **Current price:** **₹646.15** (NSE last close, ~late-May 2026 pool snapshot, exchange close). Alternate ₹637.25 (CIQ Current-Cap snapshot, basis Mar-31-2026) exists; the two differ ~1.4% and CIQ's own TEV uses ₹637.25 — agents reproducing CIQ multiples should note which price is embedded.

- **Shares (market cap):** **149.30M** (paid-up capital ₹1,493.0M ÷ ₹10; corroborated by CIQ divisor and institutional-ownership back-out). Do NOT use the 144.67M CIQ "filing-date" field.
- **Shares (per-share fair value):** **151.79M** fully diluted (149.30M + 2.48M options via TSM; no converts).
- **Market cap:** **₹96,461.1M (₹96.46 bn)** on ₹646.15. (CIQ-price memo: ₹95,142.8M on ₹637.25.)
- **Net debt:** **₹11,944.4M** (total debt − cash, incl. leases; CLAUDE.md default). Company ex-lease net debt ₹3,387M; company incl-lease ₹11,636M.
- **Enterprise value:** **₹109,187.9M (₹109.19 bn)** on ₹646.15. (CIQ-price memo: ₹107,869.6M, ties to CIQ TEV exactly.) EV ex-lease (cross-check): ₹100,938.4M.
- **Key caveats to propagate:** (1) two pool prices differ ~1.4% — market cap/EV carry a ~1.4% price-snapshot band; (2) leases are inside debt/EV/net-debt — EV/EBITDA must stay post-Ind AS 116 to be consistent; (3) TSM dilution uses FY25 option count (FY26 not refreshed) — fully diluted 151.79M has <~1% uncertainty; (4) FY26 figures are board-approved-deck + CIQ "Press Release" grade (full statutory FY26 statements not yet in pool), though every capital-structure line cross-ties to the audited deck.

## VAL-02 · multiples-own-history — Multiples — Own History — HCG

### Multiples — Own History — HCG

**Company:** HealthCare Global Enterprises Limited (NSE: HCG, BSE: 539787) — pan-India oncology-focused hospital network.
**Reporting currency:** INR (₹). Amounts in ₹ million (₹ Mn) unless per-share. Fiscal year ends 31 March; FY26 = year ended 31 Mar 2026; FY27 = year ending 31 Mar 2027.
**Business type (from `00_valuation-data-triage`):** Operating company (Health Care Facilities). Per the Business-Type Method Map, EV-based multiples (EV/EBITDA, EV/EBIT, EV/Sales) and P/E are valid; the EV bridge is value-relevant. P/B is shown as a secondary cross-check (asset-heavy hospital real estate), not as a primary financial-issuer lens.
**Date:** 2026-06-01.

> **Anchor numbers (from `01_price-and-capital-structure.md`, used verbatim):** Price **₹646.15** (NSE last close, ~late-May 2026 pool snapshot); market-cap shares **149.30M**; fully diluted (per-share) shares **151.79M**; market cap **₹96,461.1M**; net debt **₹11,944.4M** (total debt − cash, incl. leases); minority interest **₹782.4M**; **EV ₹109,187.9M** (leases-in-debt, anchor price). CIQ reproduces its own TEV ₹107,869.6M on its ₹637.25 price — used only for tie-outs below.
>
> **Cross-module inputs used:** `earnings/01_historical-financials.md` (FY22–FY26 metric base: revenue, Reported/Adjusted EBITDA, EBIT, EPS, FCF) and `earnings/04_guidance-consensus.md` (FY27/NTM consensus: revenue ₹29,105.9M, EBITDA ₹5,737.3M, EBIT ₹3,145.8M, EPS Normalized ₹8.72). Both available and consumed, not re-derived.

---

#### 1. Current Multiples

EV-based multiples use the anchor EV (₹109,187.9M); equity multiples use the anchor price (₹646.15) and the relevant per-share base. Reporting currency INR. **Lease note (from `01`):** EV includes ₹8,249.5M of Ind AS 116 lease liabilities and FY26 EBITDA is post-Ind AS 116 (rent below the line), so EV/EBITDA is internally consistent on a leases-in basis — do not pair this EV with a pre-IFRS-16 EBITDA.

| Multiple | Basis (LTM / NTM / FY) | Metric Value | Current Multiple | Source |
|---|---|---:|---:|---|
| P / E (reported) | LTM (FY26) | EPS ₹0.96 (GAAP diluted) | **~673x (effectively NM)** | Key Stats Diluted EPS FY26; price ₹646.15 |
| P / E (normalized) | LTM (FY26) | EPS-N ₹3.59 | **180x** | `earnings/04` surprise table (actual EPS-N FY26 ₹3.59) |
| P / E (normalized) | NTM (FY27) | EPS-N ₹8.72 | **74.1x** | `earnings/04`; multiples.xls FY27 P/E 74.08 ✓ |
| EV / EBITDA (reported) | LTM (FY26) | EBITDA ₹4,657.9M | **23.4x** | EV ₹109,187.9M ÷ 4,657.9; ties CIQ Jun-26 close 23.37x |
| EV / EBITDA (reported) | NTM (FY27) | EBITDA ₹5,737.3M | **19.0x** | EV ÷ 5,737.3; multiples.xls FY27 19.03 ✓ |
| EV / EBIT | LTM (FY26) | EBIT ₹2,466M (earnings basis) | **44.3x** | EV ÷ 2,466 (`earnings/01`). CIQ uses EBIT ₹2,216.2M → 49.3x — see note |
| EV / EBIT | NTM (FY27) | EBIT ₹3,145.8M | **34.7x** | EV ÷ 3,145.8; multiples.xls FY27 34.71 ✓ |
| EV / Sales | LTM (FY26) | Revenue ₹25,454.1M | **4.29x** | EV ÷ 25,454.1; ties CIQ Jun-26 close 4.29x |
| EV / Sales | NTM (FY27) | Revenue ₹29,105.9M | **3.75x** | EV ÷ 29,105.9; multiples.xls FY27 3.75 ✓ |
| P / Book | LTM (FY26) | BVPS ₹87.75 (fully diluted) | **7.36x** | price ÷ 87.75 (book equity ₹13,319.7M ÷ 151.79M) |
| P / Tangible Book | LTM (FY26) | TBVPS ₹60.05 (fully diluted) | **10.76x** | price ÷ 60.05 (TBV ₹9,115.1M ÷ 151.79M) |
| P / FCF (FCF yield) | LTM (FY26) | FCF ₹586M | **164.6x (0.61% yield)** | mkt cap ₹96,461.1M ÷ 586; FCF = CFO 3,471 − capex 2,885 |
| Dividend yield | LTM | DPS ₹0.00 | **0.00%** | Financials.xls IS "Dividends per Share = NA" all years; nil dividend |

**Reported vs adjusted (required).** EV/EBITDA above uses **Reported EBITDA** (₹4,657.9M). On **Adjusted EBITDA** (₹4,711.3M; + one-time ₹39M + ESOP ₹14M) it is **23.18x** — a ~0.3x difference, immaterial to the read. P/E on **reported** GAAP EPS (₹0.96) is mechanically ~673x and not meaningful because FY26 PAT was crushed by a ₹319M goodwill impairment and a ₹127M labour-code charge (`earnings/01`, `04`); the **normalized** EPS (₹3.59) gives 180x LTM, and the forward NTM figure (74x) is the usable equity multiple. Treat reported LTM P/E as NM and anchor the equity read on NTM.

**EBIT-base note.** Two EBIT figures exist for FY26: `earnings/01` operating EBIT **₹2,466M** (Reported EBITDA − D&A ₹2,442M, pre other-income) vs CIQ Key Stats EBIT **₹2,216.2M** (a narrower CIQ construct). I show EV/EBIT on the earnings-module basis (44.3x) as primary and flag CIQ's 49.3x; this gap does not affect the EV/EBITDA or EV/Sales reads, which are the anchors here.

---

#### 2. Historical Multiple Bands

Two complementary history sources exist in the pool; both are shown because each has a limitation, and neither alone is a clean 3–5 year intraday band.

**Source A — CIQ Multiples sheet (cleanest like-for-like, but short: 6 quarters, Mar-2025 → Jun-2026).** Quarterly LTM/NTM TEV-based and price-based multiples, "Close" basis (point-in-time at each quarter-end), current = the Jun-2026 (serial 46174) column. This is the most directly comparable series to the current multiples in Section 1, but it spans only ~15 months — **it does not capture a full 3–5 year cycle.**

| Multiple (CIQ 6-qtr, close) | Min | Mean | Median | Max | Current | Percentile of Range |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| EV / EBITDA — LTM | 21.89 | 24.20 | 24.48 | 25.67 | **23.37** | 39% |
| EV / EBITDA — NTM | 17.19 | 19.29 | 19.32 | 21.19 | **19.03** | 46% |
| EV / EBIT — LTM | 45.17 | 51.71 | 51.37 | 57.95 | **48.95** | 30% |
| EV / EBIT — NTM | 31.25 | 35.42 | 36.35 | 38.16 | **34.71** | 50% |
| EV / Sales — LTM | 3.83 | 4.27 | 4.22 | 4.60 | **4.29** | 60% |
| EV / Sales — NTM | 3.24 | 3.65 | 3.72 | 3.92 | **3.75** | 75% |
| P / E — NTM | 65.80 | 82.98 | 81.86 | 100.84 | **74.08** | 24% |
| P / Book | 7.02 | 8.64 | 8.53 | 10.09 | **7.02** | 0% (series low) |
| P / Tangible Book | 10.26 | 16.18 | 16.81 | 20.65 | **10.26** | 0% (series low) |

*Source: Financials.xls "Multiples" sheet, quarterly close, data as of 2026-06-01. Realized intraquarter extremes are wider — e.g., TEV/LTM EBITDA touched 20.78 (low) to 29.79 (high) across the six quarters; TEV/NTM EBITDA 16.31 to 24.87.*

**Source B — Historical Capitalization annual TEV (longer ~4 years, but point-in-time at the Sept filing date, and CIQ uses a narrower EBITDA definition).** CIQ's annual TEV is computed at the filing-date price each September (FY22→Sep-2022 price ₹271.2; FY25→Sep-2025 price ₹694.25). Pairing it with the FY metric base gives a longer EV/EBITDA history, shown on **both** the CIQ-narrow EBITDA and the earnings-module Reported EBITDA so the definition gap is visible.

| Multiple (annual, Sept-pricing) | FY22 | FY23 | FY24 | FY25 | Mean | Median | Current (anchor) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| EV / Sales | 3.22 | 3.24 | 3.35 | 5.05 | 3.71 | 3.29 | **4.29** (LTM) |
| EV / EBITDA (Reported/earn basis) | 18.32 | 18.35 | 19.42 | 29.01 | 21.28 | 18.89 | **23.44** (LTM rep) |
| EV / EBITDA (CIQ-narrow basis) | 25.19 | 23.11 | 23.96 | 35.91 | 27.04 | 24.58 | **23.44** (anchor) |

*Source: Financials.xls "Historical Capitalization" (TEV at filing-date price) ÷ FY revenue & EBITDA. The FY25 snapshot is the high-water mark: at the Sep-2025 price (₹694.25, above today's ₹646.15) on FY25 EBITDA, EV/EBITDA was ~29x (reported basis) / ~36x (CIQ basis) — the stock had re-rated sharply while EBITDA was still ramping. Treat the FY25 column as the recent peak, not a normal mid-cycle reading.*

**Read across the two sources:** the genuinely usable multi-year band is short. Source A is clean but only 6 quarters; Source B is ~4 years but is a once-a-year snapshot on a different EBITDA definition. Neither supports a precise "5-year mean." The defensible statement is a **range**: HCG's EV/EBITDA has traded roughly **18–29x (reported basis)** / **23–36x (CIQ basis)** over FY22–FY26, and **22–26x LTM (close)** in the last six quarters.

---

#### 3. Re-Rating / De-Rating Read

**EV-based multiples: mid-to-low range versus the last six quarters; below the FY25 peak but above the FY22–FY24 norm.** On the cleanest like-for-like window (Source A), current LTM EV/EBITDA (23.4x) sits at the **39th percentile**, **−3.4% below its own 6-quarter mean (24.20x)** and **−4.6% below the median (24.48x)**; LTM EV/EBIT (48.95x) is at the 30th percentile (−5.3% vs mean); EV/Sales (4.29x LTM, 60th percentile) is essentially **at its own mean (+0.6%)**. So on operating multiples the stock has **de-rated modestly off its early-2025 highs and now sits mid-range** — not stretched, not washed out. Against the longer Source B annual history, however, current EV/EBITDA (23.4x reported) is **above** the FY22–FY24 cluster (~18–19x) and only below the FY25 snapshot peak (~29x) — i.e., versus a 4-year lens the operating multiple is **above its own historical centre**, which the 6-quarter window alone hides.

**Equity multiples: at the bottom of the range — but mechanically, not because the market re-priced quality down.** Current P/Book (7.02x) and P/Tangible Book (10.26x) are both at the **0th percentile (series low)**, −18.8% and −36.6% below their 6-quarter means. This is **not** a clean de-rating signal: the ₹4,250M rights issue (completed Q4 FY26, oversubscribed 1.3x; `earnings/01`, `01`) lifted book value per share from ~₹59 (FY25, ~139.4M shares) to ~₹88 (FY26, 149.3M shares) while the price was roughly flat — so P/BV reset to the series low **arithmetically**, because the book denominator jumped, not because the equity was marked down. Likewise the reported LTM P/E spiking to ~673x reflects the FY26 PAT collapse (₹319M goodwill impairment + ₹127M labour-code charge + higher D&A/finance cost), not a multiple the market is actually paying — which is why the forward P/E (74x NTM, 24th percentile) is the honest equity reading and itself sits below its own 6-quarter mean.

**Most likely reason for the spread:** leverage and depreciation, not sentiment. The capex/expansion phase (Nagpur, Vizag, North Bangalore; D&A ₹2,442M, finance cost ₹1,766M in FY26 — `earnings/01`) compressed earnings-based equity multiples while the EV multiples (which sit above the earnings hit) held mid-range. The rights issue then re-set book value. The net effect is a **divergence between near-fair EV multiples and floor-level equity multiples that is mechanical** — a leverage/D&A/share-count story, not a market re-rating of business quality.

---

#### 4. Implied Value from Reversion

Equity value = implied EV − net debt (₹11,944.4M) − minority interest (₹782.4M); per share ÷ 151.79M fully diluted (bridge ties: 109,187.9 − 11,944.4 − 782.4 = ₹96,461.1M = anchor market cap ✓). Equity multiples (P/E, P/BV) applied directly to the per-share base. **Reversion targets are taken from the Source-A 6-quarter "Close" bands** (the cleanest like-for-like window); the longer annual EV/EBITDA mean/median is added as a lower-bound cross-check.

| Multiple | Reversion Target (mean / median) | Metric Applied | Implied EV / Equity (₹Mn) | Implied Price/Share | vs Current (₹646.15) |
|---|---:|---|---:|---:|---:|
| EV/EBITDA LTM (6-qtr) | 24.20x mean / 24.48x median | EBITDA ₹4,657.9M (LTM) | EV 112,721 / Eq 99,994 → ... | ₹659 / ₹667 | +2.0% / +3.3% |
| EV/EBITDA NTM (6-qtr) | 19.29x mean / 19.32x median | EBITDA ₹5,737.3M (FY27) | EV 110,672 / Eq 97,945 → ... | ₹645 / ₹646 | −0.1% / +0.0% |
| EV/Sales LTM (6-qtr) | 4.27x mean / 4.22x median | Revenue ₹25,454.1M (LTM) | EV 108,689 / Eq 95,962 → ... | ₹632 / ₹624 | −2.2% / −3.5% |
| P/E NTM (6-qtr) | 82.98x mean / 81.86x median | EPS-N ₹8.72 (FY27) | direct | ₹724 / ₹714 | +12.0% / +10.5% |
| P/Book (6-qtr) | 8.64x mean / 8.53x median | BVPS ₹87.75 (LTM) | direct | ₹758 / ₹749 | +17.3% / +15.8% |
| *EV/EBITDA annual (FY22-25), cross-check* | *21.28x mean / 18.89x median* | *EBITDA ₹4,657.9M (LTM rep)* | *EV 99,120 / Eq 86,393 — EV 87,988 / Eq 75,261* | *₹569 / ₹496* | *−11.9% / −23.3%* |

**Implied value as a RANGE:** reverting the operating multiples (EV/EBITDA and EV/Sales, the anchors for a hospital operator) to their own 6-quarter mean/median implies **≈₹624–₹667/share — within roughly ±3.5% of the current ₹646.15** (the stock is already close to its own recent EV-multiple centre). The equity multiples imply more upside (P/E NTM ₹714–₹724; P/BV ₹749–₹758), and the longer annual EV/EBITDA implies downside (₹496–₹569). **Full reversion range across all multiples: ≈₹496–₹758.** The tight cluster on the operating multiples (₹624–₹667) is the highest-confidence read; the wider tails depend on which history and which multiple you trust.

**Reversion assumption — and whether evidence supports it (required).** Reverting to mean assumes the **warranted multiple has not structurally changed.** Two of the rows fail that test and should be discounted:
- **P/BV reversion to 8.5–8.6x is NOT warranted.** The mean was set when book value was ~₹59/share pre-rights; the ₹4,250M rights issue permanently raised book to ~₹88/share, so the old P/BV mean is computed on a different (smaller) equity base. Applying it to today's larger book double-counts the equity raise — the ₹749–₹758 implied price is overstated for that reason.
- **P/E NTM reversion to ~82x carries the EPS-estimate risk flagged in `earnings/04`.** FY27 EPS-N (₹8.72) has been cut −19% over six months and HCG has missed normalized-EPS consensus in three of the last four fiscal years; the ~82x mean was struck on higher EPS estimates, so the ₹714–₹724 implied price rests on both a high multiple and a still-falling earnings base.
- **EV/EBITDA reversion is the most defensible** because EBITDA (the operating line) has been stable-to-rising and lands within ~1.5% of consensus, and the multiple has not structurally moved (still 22–26x LTM). But note the **direction depends on the window**: against the 6-quarter mean it implies ~+2–3%; against the longer FY22–FY25 mean/median (18–21x) it implies **−12% to −23%**, because the stock re-rated upward through FY25 and the current multiple sits above its 4-year centre. That window-dependence is itself the finding — there is no single "the mean" to revert to.

---

#### 5. Own-History Read

1. Against its own **last six quarters**, HCG trades **mid-to-low range on operating multiples** — LTM EV/EBITDA 23.4x is −3.4% below its own mean and at the 39th percentile, and EV/Sales is essentially at its own mean — so reverting the EV multiples to their recent centre implies only ≈₹624–₹667 (within ±3.5% of the ₹646.15 price); the stock is **near its own recent EV-multiple fair value, not stretched and not washed out.**

2. The **equity multiples sit at series lows** (P/BV 7.0x and P/Tangible Book 10.3x both at the 0th percentile, reported LTM P/E effectively NM at ~673x), but this is **mechanical, not a quality re-rating**: the ₹4,250M rights issue lifted book per share from ~₹59 to ~₹88, and FY26 PAT was gutted by a ₹319M goodwill impairment, a ₹127M labour-code charge, and rising D&A/finance cost — so the floor-level equity multiples reflect a bigger denominator and a one-off-hit numerator, not the market marking the business down.

3. **Single biggest caveat:** there is **no clean multi-year mean to revert to**, and the windows disagree directionally. The clean like-for-like series is only ~15 months long; the only ~4-year series is a once-a-year Sept snapshot on a narrower EBITDA definition that shows the current EV/EBITDA (23.4x) sitting **above** the FY22–FY24 norm (~18–19x) and below only the FY25 re-rating peak (~29x). Reverting to the 6-quarter mean says +2–3%; reverting to the 4-year mean says −12% to −23%. The P/BV "discount to mean" must be discarded outright because the rights issue structurally reset book value — the old P/BV mean is not a warranted target.

---

## VAL-03 · relative-valuation-peers — Relative Valuation — Peers — HCG

### Relative Valuation — Peers — HCG

*HealthCare Global Enterprises Limited (NSE: HCG, BSE: 539787) — pan-India, single-segment comprehensive cancer-care (oncology hospital) operator. Reporting currency INR (₹), all figures ₹ million (₹ Mn) unless a per-share or multiple is stated; fiscal year ends 31 March (FY26 = year ended 31 Mar 2026). Business type: **Operating company** (Health Care Facilities) per `00_valuation-data-triage` — so the EV-based multiple set (TEV/Revenue, TEV/EBITDA, TEV/EBIT, P/E, P/Tangible Book) is the correct frame per the Business-Type Method Map; this is not a bank, REIT, or holding company.*

**Anchor (from `01_price-and-capital-structure`, used verbatim):** Price **₹646.15** (NSE last close, ~late-May 2026 pool snapshot); market cap **₹96,461.1M**; EV **₹109,187.9M**; net debt **₹11,944.4M** (incl. leases); shares (market cap) **149.30M**, shares (per-share fair value) **151.79M** fully diluted. Net debt = total debt ₹17,353.6M − cash ₹5,409.2M; minority interest ₹782.4M.

> **Cross-module inputs used (all present):** `business-model/08_competitive-map.md` (named peer set), `business-model/07_business-quality.md` and `business-model/09_moat.md` (warranted-multiple argument), `earnings/01_historical-financials.md` (HCG metrics), `valuation/01_price-and-capital-structure.md` (anchor). The peer set is **vendor/web-assembled, not company-named** — HCG's filings name no corporate competitor in a competition section (see §1 caveat).

> **One critical price/FX note that propagates through this report.** The Capital IQ comparable export that supplies every peer multiple is denominated in **US Dollars** and embeds HCG's own price at **$6.82 ≈ ₹637.25** (the CIQ-snapshot price, basis Mar-31-2026), *not* the ₹646.15 anchor. The implied INR/USD rate from HCG's own line is **≈₹94.7/USD** (rev: 25,454 ÷ 268.7 = 94.73; EBITDA: 4,658 ÷ 49.2 = 94.67). At that rate the export's HCG market cap ($1,018.3M → ₹96,464M) and TEV ($1,152.6M → ₹109,186M) reproduce the INR anchor to within ~0.1%, so the export and the anchor describe the same company. HCG's *own* multiples in the table below are therefore on the ₹637.25 basis; on the ₹646.15 anchor (~1.4% higher) HCG's EV multiples would be ~1.4% richer and its discounts to peers ~1.4% shallower. This does not change any verdict (the gaps are far larger than 1.4%) but it is flagged so no downstream agent double-counts the price difference.

---

#### 1. Peer Set

The set is the **Capital IQ relevancy-ranked comparable export** in the data pool (`Company Comparable Analysis HealthCare Global Enterprises Limited.xls`, Trading Multiples / Operating Statistics / Financial Data sheets, as-of 2026-06-01), cross-checked against the named rivals in `08_competitive-map.md`. **It is self-selected / vendor-selected, not company-named:** HCG's filings name only Tata Memorial (an R&D peer), so no issuer-disclosed competitor list exists [08_competitive-map §2, §5]. The export is a set of **listed Indian (plus two non-Indian) multi-specialty hospital operators that carry an oncology line** — it is the best available public comp set, but with an important limitation: **none is a pure-play oncology chain like HCG.** HCG's two truest like-for-like oncology rivals (Apollo's oncology vertical; private American Oncology Institute) cannot be compared on multiples (see "private/unlistable peers" below).

| Peer | Ticker | Why Comparable | Source of Inclusion |
|---|---|---|---|
| Max Healthcare | BSE:543220 / NSE:MAXHEALTH | Listed multi-specialty chain with a named oncology line; the largest, highest-margin operator in the set; North-India weighted vs HCG's South/West tilt. The set's margin/scale benchmark. | CIQ comparable export + `08_competitive-map §2` (named) |
| Fortis Healthcare | NSE:FORTIS | Listed multi-specialty chain, oncology among specialties; ~3.6x HCG revenue. | CIQ comparable export + `08_competitive-map §2` (named bloc) |
| Narayana Hrudayalaya | NSE:NH | Listed multi-specialty; runs an oncology line; broadly comparable scale (~3.1x HCG rev). | CIQ comparable export + `08_competitive-map §2` (named bloc) |
| Aster DM Healthcare | NSE:ASTERDM | Listed multi-specialty, India + GCC; oncology line; ~1.8x HCG rev. | CIQ comparable export + `08_competitive-map §2` (named bloc) |
| Global Health (Medanta) | NSE:MEDANTA | Listed multi-specialty, "cardiac and cancer care"; ~1.7x HCG rev. | CIQ comparable export + `08_competitive-map §2` (named bloc) |
| Krishna Institute (KIMS) | NSE:KIMS | Listed multi-specialty, oncology among specialties; ~1.5x HCG rev. | CIQ comparable export + `08_competitive-map §2` (named bloc) |
| Rainbow Children's Medicare | NSE:RAINBOW | Listed single-specialty (paediatrics) hospital chain — a useful *single-specialty* comp (similar focused model, different specialty); ~0.7x HCG rev. | CIQ comparable export (relevancy-ranked) |
| Yatharth Hospital & Trauma | NSE:YATHARTH | Listed multi-specialty / trauma hospital chain; smaller (~0.5x HCG rev). | CIQ comparable export (relevancy-ranked) |
| Sanbo Hospital Management | SZSE:301293 | Chinese listed hospital operator; included by CIQ relevancy; **flagged** — different country, regulatory regime, and an outlier on several multiples. | CIQ comparable export (relevancy-ranked) |
| Medical Facilities Corp | TSX:DR | Canadian surgical-facility operator; included by CIQ relevancy; **flagged** — different country/model and the set's low-multiple outlier (TEV/EBITDA 3.4x). | CIQ comparable export (relevancy-ranked) |

**Private / unlistable peers (no public multiples — flagged, not guessed):**
- **American Oncology Institute (AOI / CTSI)** — HCG's *closest like-for-like rival* (standalone single-specialty cancer chain, ~18 hospitals, Tier-2 weighted), but **private** (CTSI/Asia Healthcare Holdings; CTSI acquired by Varian/Siemens). No public multiple exists; cannot be placed in the table [08_competitive-map §2].
- **Apollo Cancer Centres (within Apollo Hospitals, NSE:APOLLOHOSP)** — the deepest-pocketed oncology rival (14 dedicated centres + proton therapy), but Apollo does **not** break out a stand-alone oncology revenue/EBITDA line, and Apollo is **not carried in HCG's CIQ relevancy-ranked export**. A group-level Apollo multiple would mix oncology with a large non-oncology business, so it is excluded rather than forced in [08_competitive-map §2; 09_moat §3].

**Honest read on comparability:** the multiples below benchmark HCG against *listed multi-specialty hospitals*, not against *pure-play oncology economics* — the two oncology-specific rivals are off-screen. The two non-Indian names (Sanbo, Medical Facilities Corp) are kept because CIQ ranked them in, but they are flagged as outliers and the verdict leans on the eight Indian names. Where it matters, the read is sanity-checked against the **median excluding the two non-India outliers** (footnoted in §2).

---

#### 2. Peer Multiples & Operating Stats

All figures from the **Capital IQ comparable export, as-of 2026-06-01** (Trading Multiples + Operating Statistics + Financial Data sheets), denominated in USD, converted at "today's spot rate" by CIQ. Multiple definitions (CIQ default): **TEV/Rev, TEV/EBITDA, TEV/EBIT = LTM-Latest**; **P/E = Price / Diluted EPS before extra, LTM-Latest**; **P/TangBV = LTM**; growth and margins = **LTM**; **Total Debt/EBITDA = LTM**. HCG's line is on the export's ₹637.25 / $6.82 price (see anchor note). **FCF yield is NOT carried in the export for any company** — it is shown for HCG only (computed from filings) and marked "n/a (peer)" for the rest rather than fabricated. **ROIC is NOT carried for peers** in the export (confirmed in `09_moat §3`); HCG's own Return on Capital is shown from the CIQ Financials export and peers are marked "n/d".

| Company | P/E (LTM) | EV/EBITDA (LTM) | EV/EBIT (LTM) | EV/Sales (LTM) | FCF Yield | Rev Growth (LTM) | EBITDA Margin | ROIC | Net Debt/EBITDA¹ | Data As-of |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| **HCG** | **NM²** | **23.4** | **49.0** | **4.3** | **0.6%³** | **+14.5%** | **18.3%** | **4.6%⁴** | **2.6x** | CIQ export 2026-06-01 |
| Krishna Institute (KIMS) | 125.0 | 43.1 | 66.4 | 8.9 | n/a (peer) | +28.6% | 20.5% | n/d | 5.2x | CIQ export 2026-06-01 |
| Global Health (Medanta) | 56.6 | 34.3 | 45.2 | 7.1 | n/a (peer) | +19.4% | 20.8% | n/d | 1.3x | CIQ export 2026-06-01 |
| Narayana Hrudayalaya | 47.7 | 26.0 | 36.1 | 5.3 | n/a (peer) | +44.0% | 20.5% | n/d | 3.5x | CIQ export 2026-06-01 |
| Sanbo (SZSE, flagged)⁵ | 200.0 | 54.0 | 107.7 | 7.0 | n/a (peer) | +19.6% | 11.5% | n/d | 3.1x | CIQ export 2026-06-01 |
| Yatharth Hospital | 43.6 | 24.1 | 33.3 | 6.2 | n/a (peer) | +39.2% | 25.8% | n/d | 0.8x | CIQ export 2026-06-01 |
| Rainbow Children's | 50.0 | 26.4 | 36.5 | 8.4 | n/a (peer) | +12.3% | 32.0% | n/d | 1.6x | CIQ export 2026-06-01 |
| Aster DM Healthcare | 95.9 | 44.5 | 64.0 | 8.3 | n/a (peer) | +12.2% | 19.4% | n/d | 2.5x | CIQ export 2026-06-01 |
| Max Healthcare | 63.6 | 42.0 | 52.4 | 11.2 | n/a (peer) | +19.1% | 26.8% | n/d | 1.6x | CIQ export 2026-06-01 |
| Medical Facilities (TSX, flagged)⁵ | 17.1 | 3.4 | 4.3 | 0.8 | n/a (peer) | +16.1% | 20.9% | n/d | 0.9x | CIQ export 2026-06-01 |
| Fortis Healthcare | 67.3 | 34.9 | 44.4 | 8.0 | n/a (peer) | +17.3% | 22.8% | n/d | 1.7x | CIQ export 2026-06-01 |
| **Peer median (10 peers)** | **60.1** | **34.6** | **44.8** | **7.6** | **n/a** | **+19.3%** | **20.9%** | **n/d** | **1.7x** | recomputed; ties to CIQ |
| **Peer mean (10 peers)** | **76.7** | **33.3** | **49.0** | **7.1** | **n/a** | **+22.8%** | **22.1%** | **n/d** | **2.2x** | recomputed; ties to CIQ |
| *Median ex-Sanbo & MedFac (8 India peers)⁶* | *63.6* | *34.9* | *44.8* | *8.0* | *n/a* | *+19.3%* | *21.6%* | *n/d* | *1.7x* | recomputed |
